Resolution for year encoding (not used).

season_size: int = 293

Size of the season encoder (total bits).

season_active_bits: int = 6

Set to greater than zero to enable season encoding. Number of active bits for season (day of year). how many bits to apply to season Member: season - The portion of the year. Unit is day. Range is 0 to 366 (to avoid leap year issues).

season_sparsity: float = 0.0

Sparsity for season encoding (not used).

season_radius: float = 73.25

Radius for season encoding, in days (default ~4 seasons) days per season.

season_resolution: float = 0.0

Resolution for season encoding (not used).

day_of_week_size: int = 293

Size of the day of week encoder (total bits).

day_of_week_active_bits: int = 6

Set to greater than zero to enable day of week encoding. Number of active bits for day of week, how many bits to apply to day of week.

day_of_week_sparsity: float = 0.0

Sparsity for day of week encoding (not used).

day_of_week_radius: float = 41.85

Radius for day of week encoding, every day is a separate bucket.

day_of_week_resolution: float = 0.0

Resolution for day of week encoding (not used).

weekend_size: int = 293

Size of the weekend encoder (total bits).

weekend_active_bits: int = 6

Set to greater than zero to enable weekend encoding. Number of active bits for weekend flag.

weekend_sparsity: float = 0.0

Sparsity for weekend encoding (not used).

weekend_radius: float = 1.0

Radius for weekend encoding (not used).

weekend_resolution: float = 0.0

Resolution for weekend encoding (not used).

holiday_size: int = 293

Size of the holiday encoder (total bits).

holiday_active_bits: int = 6

Set to greater than zero to enable holiday encoding. Number of active bits for holiday encoding.

holiday_sparsity: float = 0.0

Sparsity for holiday encoding (not used).

holiday_radius: float = 1.0

Radius for holiday encoding (not used).

holiday_resolution: float = 0.0

Resolution for holiday encoding (not used).

holiday_dates: list[list[int]]

List of holidays as [month, day] or [year, month, day].

time_of_day_size: int = 293

Size of the time of day encoder (total bits).

time_of_day_active_bits: int = 6

Set to greater than zero to enable time of day encoding. Number of active bits for time of day.

time_of_day_sparsity: float = 0.0

Sparsity for time of day encoding (not used).

time_of_day_radius: float = 12.2

Radius for time of day encoding, in hours.

time_of_day_resolution: float = 0.0

Resolution for time of day encoding (not used).

custom_size: int = 293

Size of the custom days encoder (total bits).

custom_active_bits: int = 6

Set to greater than zero to enable custom days encoding. Number of active bits for custom day groups.

custom_sparsity: float = 0.0

Sparsity for custom days encoding (not used).

custom_radius: float = 58.6

Radius for custom days encoding (not used).

custom_resolution: float = 0.0

Resolution for custom days encoding (not used).

custom_days: list[str]

List of custom day group strings (e.g., ["mon,wed,fri"]).

seed: int = 42

Random seed for encoder initialization.

rdse_used: bool = True

Enable RDSE usage for date encoder.

encoder_class = <class 'DateEncoder'>

Reference to the DateEncoder class for factory instantiation.

class RandomDistributedScalarEncoder(psu_capstone.encoder_layer.BaseEncoder[float | int]): 
 32class RandomDistributedScalarEncoder(BaseEncoder[float | int]):
 33    """Random Distributed Scalar Encoder using MurmurHash3 for stable encodings.
 34
 35    The RDSE encodes numeric scalar values into SDRs using hash-based random
 36    bit selection. It provides more flexibility than ScalarEncoder by not
 37    requiring pre-specified input ranges and determining encodings at runtime.
 38
 39    Uses MurmurHash3 algorithm for deterministic, collision-resistant encoding.
 40
 41    Args:
 42        parameters: Configuration for RDSE encoding behavior.
 43    """
 44
 45    def __init__(self, parameters: RDSEParameters):
 46        self._parameters = copy.deepcopy(parameters)
 47        self._parameters = self.check_parameters(self._parameters)
 48
 49        self._size = self._parameters.size
 50        self._active_bits = self._parameters.active_bits
 51        self._sparsity = self._parameters.sparsity
 52        self._radius = self._parameters.radius
 53        self._resolution = self._parameters.resolution
 54        self._category = self._parameters.category
 55        self._seed = self._parameters.seed
 56        self._encoding_cache: dict[float, list[int]] = {}
 57        self.knn: KNeighborsRegressor
 58        self.encoding: bool = False
 59
 60        self.logger = get_logger(self)
 61
 62        super().__init__(self._size)
 63
 64    @override
 65    def encode(self, input_value: float | int) -> list[int]:
 66        """Encode the input value into a binary vector."""
 67        if type(input_value) is not int and type(input_value) is not float:
 68            raise ValueError("A scalar encoder can only encode floats or ints.")
 69        self.register_encoding(input_value)
 70        self.logger.info("RDSE encoded value: %s", input_value)
 71        return self._compute_encoding(input_value)
 72
 73    def register_encoding(
 74        self, input_value: float | int, encoded: list[int] | None = None
 75    ) -> list[int]:
 76        """Caches an encoding so decode_closest can compare against it."""
 77        vector = encoded if encoded is not None else self._compute_encoding(input_value)
 78        if len(vector) != self.size:
 79            raise ValueError("Stored encoding must be the same length as encoder size")
 80        self._encoding_cache[input_value] = vector
 81        return vector
 82
 83    def clear_registered_encodings(self) -> None:
 84        """Clears cached encodings used for nearest-neighbor decoding."""
 85        self._encoding_cache.clear()
 86
 87    def _compute_encoding(self, input_value: float) -> list[int]:
 88        """
 89        Uses murmurhash3 algorithm to encode a float value.
 90
 91        :param self: Description
 92        :param input_value: The value we want encoded.
 93        :type input_value: float
 94        :return: Returns a list of integers that signify an SDR.
 95        :rtype: list[int]
 96        """
 97        if self._category:
 98            if input_value != int(input_value) or input_value < 0:
 99                raise ValueError("Input to category encoder must be an unsigned integer")
100        self.encoding = True
101        data = [0] * self.size
102
103        index = int(input_value / self._resolution)
104
105        for offset in range(self._active_bits):
106            hash_buffer = index + offset
107
108            """
109                The lower case i in the struct.pack makes this take in signed 32 bit integers.
110                It is important to note the previous iteration used an upper case I which
111                made this not take negative values. The struct.pack converts an integer
112                into a byte representation.
113            """
114            bucket = mmh3.hash(struct.pack("i", hash_buffer), self._seed, signed=False)
115            bucket = bucket % self.size
116            """
117                Don't worry about hash collisions.  Instead measure the critical
118                properties of the encoder in unit tests and quantify how significant
119                the hash collisions are.  This encoder can not fix the collisions
120                because it does not record past encodings.  Collisions cause small
121                deviations in the sparsity or semantic similarity, depending on how
122                they're handled.
123            """
124            data[bucket] = 1
125        return data
126
127    @staticmethod
128    def _overlap(first: list[int], second: list[int]) -> int:
129        """
130        Checks for overlapping bits in two Lists of integers.
131
132        :param first: The first list in checking.
133        :type first: list[int]
134        :param second: The second list in checking.
135        :type second: list[int]
136        :return: Returns the overlapping bits.
137        :rtype: int
138        """
139        if len(first) != len(second):
140            raise ValueError("Vectors must be the same length to compute overlap")
141        return sum(1 for a, b in zip(first, second) if a == 1 and b == 1)
142
143    def sparsify(self, vector: list[int]) -> list[int]:
144        """Converts a sparse activity vector to a activation list."""
145        return [i for i, bit in enumerate(vector) if bit == 1]
146
147    @override
148    def decode(
149        self, encoded: list[int], candidates: Iterable[float] | None = None
150    ) -> tuple[float | None, float]:
151        """Returns the value whose encoding overlaps the most with the provided SDR."""
152        if len(encoded) != self.size:
153            raise ValueError(
154                f"Encoded input size ({len(encoded)}) does not match encoder size ({self.size})"
155            )
156
157        search_values = (
158            list(candidates) if candidates is not None else list(self._encoding_cache.keys())
159        )
160        if not search_values:
161            raise ValueError("No candidate encodings available for decoding")
162
163        best_value: float | None = None
164        best_overlap = -1
165
166        for candidate in search_values:
167            candidate_encoding = self._encoding_cache.get(candidate)
168            if candidate_encoding is None:
169                candidate_encoding = self.register_encoding(candidate)
170            overlap = self._overlap(encoded, candidate_encoding)
171            if overlap > best_overlap:
172                best_overlap = overlap
173                best_value = candidate
174
175        confidence = (
176            best_overlap / self._active_bits if best_overlap >= 0 and self._active_bits else 0.0
177        )
178        self.logger.info("Decoded SDR into value: %s, with confidence: %s", best_value, confidence)
179        return best_value, confidence
180
181    def make_knn(self) -> None:
182        """
183        Alternate method to decode where it employs a knn regressor model.
184
185        :param self: Description
186        """
187        if not self._encoding_cache:
188            raise ValueError("No registered encodings available to build KNN")
189
190        x = np.array(list(self._encoding_cache.values()), dtype=np.uint8)
191        y = np.array(list(self._encoding_cache.keys()), dtype=np.float32)
192
193        knn = KNeighborsRegressor(
194            n_neighbors=min(2, len(y)),
195            weights="distance",
196            metric="hamming",
197        )
198        knn.fit(x, y)
199        self.knn = knn
200
201    def decode_knn(self, encoded: list[int]) -> float:
202        """Returns the value whose encoding overlaps the most with the provided SDR."""
203        if len(encoded) != self.size:
204            raise ValueError("Encoded input must match encoder size")
205        if self.encoding:
206            self.make_knn()
207            self.encoding = False
208        query = np.asarray(encoded, dtype=np.int8).reshape(1, -1)
209        result = self.knn.predict(query)
210        return result.item()
211
212    # After encode we may need a check_parameters method since most of the encoders have this
213    def check_parameters(self, parameters: "RDSEParameters"):
214        """Method to check mutually exclusive parameters and fill in missing values.
215
216        Args:
217            parameters: The parameters to check and fill in.
218
219        Returns:
220            The checked and filled in parameters.
221
222        Raises:
223            ValueError: If the parameters are invalid.
224        """
225        # Check size parameter
226        if not parameters.size > 0:
227            raise ValueError("You have no size set.")
228
229        num_active_args = 0
230
231        # Check active bits / sparsity mutual exclusivity
232
233        if parameters.active_bits > 0:
234            num_active_args += 1
235        if parameters.sparsity > 0.0:
236            num_active_args += 1
237
238        if num_active_args == 0:
239            raise ValueError("Missing argument, need one of: 'activeBits' or 'sparsity'.")
240        if num_active_args != 1:
241            raise ValueError("Too many arguments, choose only one of: 'activeBits' or 'sparsity'.")
242
243        # Check radius / resolution / category mutual exclusivity
244        num_resolution_args = 0
245        if parameters.radius > 0.0:
246            num_resolution_args += 1
247        if parameters.category:
248            num_resolution_args += 1
249        if parameters.resolution > 0.0:
250            num_resolution_args += 1
251
252        if num_resolution_args == 0:
253            raise ValueError("Missing argument, need one of: 'radius', 'resolution', 'category'.")
254        if num_resolution_args != 1:
255            raise ValueError(
256                "Too many arguments, choose only one of: 'radius', 'resolution', 'category'."
257            )
258
259        # Finish filling in all of parameters.
260
261        # Determine number of activeBits.
262        if parameters.sparsity > 0:
263            if not 0 <= parameters.sparsity <= 1:
264                raise ValueError("Sparsity is not between 0 and 1 inclusive.")
265            parameters.active_bits = int(round(parameters.size * parameters.sparsity))
266            if not parameters.active_bits > 0:
267                raise ValueError("Active bits are not greater than 0.")
268
269        # Determine sparsity. Always calculate this even if it was given, to correct for rounding error.
270        parameters.sparsity = float(parameters.active_bits / parameters.size)
271
272        if parameters.category:
273            parameters.active_bits = 1
274        # Determine resolution.
275        if parameters.radius > 0.0:
276            parameters.resolution = parameters.radius / float(parameters.active_bits)
277        # Determine radius.
278        elif parameters.resolution > 0.0:
279            parameters.radius = float(parameters.active_bits) * parameters.resolution
280
281        while parameters.seed == 0:
282            parameters.seed = random.getrandbits(32)
283        return parameters

Random Distributed Scalar Encoder using MurmurHash3 for stable encodings.

The RDSE encodes numeric scalar values into SDRs using hash-based random bit selection. It provides more flexibility than ScalarEncoder by not requiring pre-specified input ranges and determining encodings at runtime.

Uses MurmurHash3 algorithm for deterministic, collision-resistant encoding.

Args: parameters: Configuration for RDSE encoding behavior.

RandomDistributedScalarEncoder(parameters: RDSEParameters) 
45    def __init__(self, parameters: RDSEParameters):
46        self._parameters = copy.deepcopy(parameters)
47        self._parameters = self.check_parameters(self._parameters)
48
49        self._size = self._parameters.size
50        self._active_bits = self._parameters.active_bits
51        self._sparsity = self._parameters.sparsity
52        self._radius = self._parameters.radius
53        self._resolution = self._parameters.resolution
54        self._category = self._parameters.category
55        self._seed = self._parameters.seed
56        self._encoding_cache: dict[float, list[int]] = {}
57        self.knn: KNeighborsRegressor
58        self.encoding: bool = False
59
60        self.logger = get_logger(self)
61
62        super().__init__(self._size)
knn: sklearn.neighbors._regression.KNeighborsRegressor
encoding: bool
logger
@override
def encode(self, input_value: float | int) -> list[int]: 
64    @override
65    def encode(self, input_value: float | int) -> list[int]:
66        """Encode the input value into a binary vector."""
67        if type(input_value) is not int and type(input_value) is not float:
68            raise ValueError("A scalar encoder can only encode floats or ints.")
69        self.register_encoding(input_value)
70        self.logger.info("RDSE encoded value: %s", input_value)
71        return self._compute_encoding(input_value)

Encode the input value into a binary vector.

def register_encoding( self, input_value: float | int, encoded: list[int] | None = None) -> list[int]: 
73    def register_encoding(
74        self, input_value: float | int, encoded: list[int] | None = None
75    ) -> list[int]:
76        """Caches an encoding so decode_closest can compare against it."""
77        vector = encoded if encoded is not None else self._compute_encoding(input_value)
78        if len(vector) != self.size:
79            raise ValueError("Stored encoding must be the same length as encoder size")
80        self._encoding_cache[input_value] = vector
81        return vector

Caches an encoding so decode_closest can compare against it.

def clear_registered_encodings(self) -> None: 
83    def clear_registered_encodings(self) -> None:
84        """Clears cached encodings used for nearest-neighbor decoding."""
85        self._encoding_cache.clear()

Clears cached encodings used for nearest-neighbor decoding.

def sparsify(self, vector: list[int]) -> list[int]: 
143    def sparsify(self, vector: list[int]) -> list[int]:
144        """Converts a sparse activity vector to a activation list."""
145        return [i for i, bit in enumerate(vector) if bit == 1]

Converts a sparse activity vector to a activation list.

@override
def decode( self, encoded: list[int], candidates: Optional[Iterable[float]] = None) -> tuple[float | None, float]: 
147    @override
148    def decode(
149        self, encoded: list[int], candidates: Iterable[float] | None = None
150    ) -> tuple[float | None, float]:
151        """Returns the value whose encoding overlaps the most with the provided SDR."""
152        if len(encoded) != self.size:
153            raise ValueError(
154                f"Encoded input size ({len(encoded)}) does not match encoder size ({self.size})"
155            )
156
157        search_values = (
158            list(candidates) if candidates is not None else list(self._encoding_cache.keys())
159        )
160        if not search_values:
161            raise ValueError("No candidate encodings available for decoding")
162
163        best_value: float | None = None
164        best_overlap = -1
165
166        for candidate in search_values:
167            candidate_encoding = self._encoding_cache.get(candidate)
168            if candidate_encoding is None:
169                candidate_encoding = self.register_encoding(candidate)
170            overlap = self._overlap(encoded, candidate_encoding)
171            if overlap > best_overlap:
172                best_overlap = overlap
173                best_value = candidate
174
175        confidence = (
176            best_overlap / self._active_bits if best_overlap >= 0 and self._active_bits else 0.0
177        )
178        self.logger.info("Decoded SDR into value: %s, with confidence: %s", best_value, confidence)
179        return best_value, confidence

Returns the value whose encoding overlaps the most with the provided SDR.

def make_knn(self) -> None: 
181    def make_knn(self) -> None:
182        """
183        Alternate method to decode where it employs a knn regressor model.
184
185        :param self: Description
186        """
187        if not self._encoding_cache:
188            raise ValueError("No registered encodings available to build KNN")
189
190        x = np.array(list(self._encoding_cache.values()), dtype=np.uint8)
191        y = np.array(list(self._encoding_cache.keys()), dtype=np.float32)
192
193        knn = KNeighborsRegressor(
194            n_neighbors=min(2, len(y)),
195            weights="distance",
196            metric="hamming",
197        )
198        knn.fit(x, y)
199        self.knn = knn

Alternate method to decode where it employs a knn regressor model.

Parameters
  • self: Description
def decode_knn(self, encoded: list[int]) -> float: 
201    def decode_knn(self, encoded: list[int]) -> float:
202        """Returns the value whose encoding overlaps the most with the provided SDR."""
203        if len(encoded) != self.size:
204            raise ValueError("Encoded input must match encoder size")
205        if self.encoding:
206            self.make_knn()
207            self.encoding = False
208        query = np.asarray(encoded, dtype=np.int8).reshape(1, -1)
209        result = self.knn.predict(query)
210        return result.item()

Returns the value whose encoding overlaps the most with the provided SDR.

def check_parameters(self, parameters: RDSEParameters): 
213    def check_parameters(self, parameters: "RDSEParameters"):
214        """Method to check mutually exclusive parameters and fill in missing values.
215
216        Args:
217            parameters: The parameters to check and fill in.
218
219        Returns:
220            The checked and filled in parameters.
221
222        Raises:
223            ValueError: If the parameters are invalid.
224        """
225        # Check size parameter
226        if not parameters.size > 0:
227            raise ValueError("You have no size set.")
228
229        num_active_args = 0
230
231        # Check active bits / sparsity mutual exclusivity
232
233        if parameters.active_bits > 0:
234            num_active_args += 1
235        if parameters.sparsity > 0.0:
236            num_active_args += 1
237
238        if num_active_args == 0:
239            raise ValueError("Missing argument, need one of: 'activeBits' or 'sparsity'.")
240        if num_active_args != 1:
241            raise ValueError("Too many arguments, choose only one of: 'activeBits' or 'sparsity'.")
242
243        # Check radius / resolution / category mutual exclusivity
244        num_resolution_args = 0
245        if parameters.radius > 0.0:
246            num_resolution_args += 1
247        if parameters.category:
248            num_resolution_args += 1
249        if parameters.resolution > 0.0:
250            num_resolution_args += 1
251
252        if num_resolution_args == 0:
253            raise ValueError("Missing argument, need one of: 'radius', 'resolution', 'category'.")
254        if num_resolution_args != 1:
255            raise ValueError(
256                "Too many arguments, choose only one of: 'radius', 'resolution', 'category'."
257            )
258
259        # Finish filling in all of parameters.
260
261        # Determine number of activeBits.
262        if parameters.sparsity > 0:
263            if not 0 <= parameters.sparsity <= 1:
264                raise ValueError("Sparsity is not between 0 and 1 inclusive.")
265            parameters.active_bits = int(round(parameters.size * parameters.sparsity))
266            if not parameters.active_bits > 0:
267                raise ValueError("Active bits are not greater than 0.")
268
269        # Determine sparsity. Always calculate this even if it was given, to correct for rounding error.
270        parameters.sparsity = float(parameters.active_bits / parameters.size)
271
272        if parameters.category:
273            parameters.active_bits = 1
274        # Determine resolution.
275        if parameters.radius > 0.0:
276            parameters.resolution = parameters.radius / float(parameters.active_bits)
277        # Determine radius.
278        elif parameters.resolution > 0.0:
279            parameters.radius = float(parameters.active_bits) * parameters.resolution
280
281        while parameters.seed == 0:
282            parameters.seed = random.getrandbits(32)
283        return parameters

Method to check mutually exclusive parameters and fill in missing values.

Args: parameters: The parameters to check and fill in.

Returns: The checked and filled in parameters.

Raises: ValueError: If the parameters are invalid.

@dataclass
class RDSEParameters: 
286@dataclass
287class RDSEParameters:
288    """Configuration parameters for :class:`RandomDistributedScalarEncoder`."""
289
290    size: int = 2048
291    """
292    * Member "size" is the total number of bits in the encoded output SDR.
293    """
294    active_bits: int = 0
295    """
296    * Member "activeBits" is the number of true bits in the encoded output SDR.
297    """
298    sparsity: float = 0.02
299    """
300    * Member "sparsity" is the fraction of bits in the encoded output which this
301    * encoder will activate. This is an alternative way to specify the member
302    * "activeBits".
303    """
304    radius: float = 0.0
305    """
306    * Member "radius" Two inputs separated by more than the radius have
307    * non-overlapping representations. Two inputs separated by less than the
308    * radius will in general overlap in at least some of their bits. You can
309    * think of this as the radius of the input.
310    """
311    resolution: float = 1.0
312    """
313    * Member "resolution" Two inputs separated by greater than, or equal to the
314    * resolution will in general have different representations.
315    """
316    category: bool = False
317    """
318    * Member "category" means that the inputs are enumerated categories.
319    * If true then this encoder will only encode unsigned integers, and all
320    * inputs will have unique / non-overlapping representations.
321    """
322    seed: int = 42
323    """
324    * Member "seed" forces different encoders to produce different outputs, even
325    * if the inputs and all other parameters are the same.  Two encoders with the
326    * same seed, parameters, and input will produce identical outputs.
327    *
328    * The seed 0 is special.  Seed 0 is replaced with a random number.
329    """
330    encoder_class = RandomDistributedScalarEncoder

Configuration parameters for RandomDistributedScalarEncoder.

RDSEParameters( size: int = 2048, active_bits: int = 0, sparsity: float = 0.02, radius: float = 0.0, resolution: float = 1.0, category: bool = False, seed: int = 42)
size: int = 2048
  • Member "size" is the total number of bits in the encoded output SDR.
active_bits: int = 0
  • Member "activeBits" is the number of true bits in the encoded output SDR.
sparsity: float = 0.02
  • Member "sparsity" is the fraction of bits in the encoded output which this
  • encoder will activate. This is an alternative way to specify the member
  • "activeBits".
radius: float = 0.0
  • Member "radius" Two inputs separated by more than the radius have
  • non-overlapping representations. Two inputs separated by less than the
  • radius will in general overlap in at least some of their bits. You can
  • think of this as the radius of the input.
resolution: float = 1.0
  • Member "resolution" Two inputs separated by greater than, or equal to the
  • resolution will in general have different representations.
category: bool = False
  • Member "category" means that the inputs are enumerated categories.
  • If true then this encoder will only encode unsigned integers, and all
  • inputs will have unique / non-overlapping representations.
seed: int = 42
  • Member "seed" forces different encoders to produce different outputs, even
  • if the inputs and all other parameters are the same. Two encoders with the
  • same seed, parameters, and input will produce identical outputs. *
  • The seed 0 is special. Seed 0 is replaced with a random number.
encoder_class = <class 'RandomDistributedScalarEncoder'>
class ScalarEncoder(psu_capstone.encoder_layer.BaseEncoder[int]): 
 26class ScalarEncoder(BaseEncoder[int]):
 27    """Encoder for numeric values using contiguous blocks of active bits.
 28
 29    The ScalarEncoder converts a numeric value into a sparse distributed
 30    representation where active bits form a contiguous block. The position
 31    of this block varies smoothly with the input value, ensuring semantic
 32    similarity - similar inputs produce overlapping representations.
 33
 34    Args:
 35        parameters: Configuration for scalar encoding behavior.
 36    """
 37
 38    def __init__(
 39        self,
 40        parameters: ScalarEncoderParameters,
 41    ):
 42        self._parameters = copy.deepcopy(parameters)
 43        self._parameters = self.check_parameters(self._parameters)
 44
 45        self._minimum = self._parameters.minimum
 46        self._maximum = self._parameters.maximum
 47        self._clip_input = self._parameters.clip_input
 48        self._periodic = self._parameters.periodic
 49        self._category = self._parameters.category
 50        self._active_bits = self._parameters.active_bits
 51        self._sparsity = self._parameters.sparsity
 52        self._size = self._parameters.size
 53        self._radius = self._parameters.radius
 54        self._resolution = self._parameters.resolution
 55        self._encoding_cache: dict[float, list[int]] = {}
 56        self.logger = get_logger(self)
 57        super().__init__(self._size)
 58
 59    """
 60        Encodes an input value into an SDR with a block of 1's.
 61
 62        Description:
 63        The encode method is responsible for transforming the supplied SDR data structure into
 64        an SDR that has the encoding of the input value.
 65    """
 66
 67    @property
 68    def size(self) -> int:
 69        return self._size
 70
 71    @size.setter
 72    def size(self, value: int) -> None:
 73
 74        self._size = value
 75
 76        try:
 77            new_params = self.check_parameters(
 78                ScalarEncoderParameters(size=self._size, radius=0.0, category=False, resolution=0.0)
 79            )
 80            self._parameters = new_params
 81
 82        except AssertionError as err:
 83            print(f"ERROR :: {self.__class__.__qualname__} :: {err}")
 84            self._size = self._parameters.size
 85
 86    def register_encoding(self, input_value: float, encoded: list[int] | None = None) -> list[int]:
 87        """Caches an encoding so decode_closest can compare against it."""
 88        vector = encoded if encoded is not None else self._compute_encoding(input_value)
 89        if len(vector) != self.size:
 90            raise ValueError("Stored encoding must be the same length as encoder size")
 91        self._encoding_cache[input_value] = vector
 92        return vector
 93
 94    def clear_registered_encodings(self) -> None:
 95        """Clears cached encodings used for nearest-neighbor decoding."""
 96        self._encoding_cache.clear()
 97
 98    def _compute_encoding(self, input_value: int | float) -> list[int]:
 99        if self._clip_input:
100            if self._periodic:
101
102                input_value = input_value % self._maximum
103            else:
104                input_value = max(input_value, self._minimum)
105                input_value = min(input_value, self._maximum)
106        else:
107            if self._category and input_value != float(int(input_value)):
108                raise ValueError("Input to category encoder must be an unsigned integer!")
109            if not (self._minimum <= input_value <= self._maximum):
110                raise ValueError(
111                    f"Input must be within range [{self._minimum}, {self._maximum}]! "
112                    f"Received {input_value}"
113                )
114
115        start = int(round((input_value - self._minimum) / self._resolution))
116
117        """Handle edge case where start + active_bits exceeds output size.
118          // The endpoints of the input range are inclusive, which means that the
119          // maximum value may round up to an index which is outside of the SDR. Correct
120          // this by pushing the endpoint (and everything which rounds to it) onto the
121          // last bit in the SDR.
122        """
123
124        if not self._periodic:
125            start = min(start, self.size - self._active_bits)
126
127        sparse: list[int] = []
128        sparse[:] = range(start, start + self._active_bits)
129
130        if self._periodic:
131            for i, bit in enumerate(sparse):
132                if bit >= self.size:
133                    sparse[i] = bit - self.size.size
134            sparse.sort()
135
136        dense = [0] * self.size
137        for idx in sparse:
138            dense[idx] = 1
139
140        return dense
141
142    @override
143    def encode(self, input_value: Any) -> list[int]:
144        """Encode the input value into a binary vector."""
145        if type(input_value) is not int and type(input_value) is not float:
146            raise ValueError("A scalar encoder can only encode floats or ints.")
147        self.register_encoding(input_value)
148        self.logger.info("Scalar encoded value: %s", input_value)
149        return self._compute_encoding(input_value)
150
151    @override
152    def decode(
153        self, encoded: list[int], candidates: Iterable[float] | None = None
154    ) -> tuple[float | None, float]:
155        """Returns the value whose encoding overlaps the most with the provided SDR."""
156        if len(encoded) != self.size:
157            raise ValueError(
158                f"Encoded input size ({len(encoded)}) does not match encoder size ({self.size})"
159            )
160
161        search_values = (
162            list(candidates) if candidates is not None else list(self._encoding_cache.keys())
163        )
164        if not search_values:
165            raise ValueError("No candidate encodings available for decoding")
166
167        best_value: float | None = None
168        best_overlap = -1
169
170        for candidate in search_values:
171            candidate_encoding = self._encoding_cache.get(candidate)
172            if candidate_encoding is None:
173                candidate_encoding = self.register_encoding(candidate)
174            overlap = self._overlap(encoded, candidate_encoding)
175            if overlap > best_overlap:
176                best_overlap = overlap
177                best_value = candidate
178
179        confidence = (
180            best_overlap / self._active_bits if best_overlap >= 0 and self._active_bits else 0.0
181        )
182        self.logger.info(
183            "Scalar decoded SDR into value: %s, with confidence: %s", best_value, confidence
184        )
185        return best_value, confidence
186
187    @staticmethod
188    def _overlap(first: list[int], second: list[int]) -> int:
189        """
190        Checks for overlapping bits in two Lists of integers.
191
192        :param first: The first list in checking.
193        :type first: List[int]
194        :param second: The second list in checking.
195        :type second: List[int]
196        :return: Returns the overlapping bits.
197        :rtype: int
198        """
199        if len(first) != len(second):
200            raise ValueError("Vectors must be the same length to compute overlap")
201        return sum(1 for a, b in zip(first, second) if a == 1 and b == 1)
202
203    def sparsify(self, vector: list[int]) -> list[int]:
204        """Converts a sparse activity vector to a activation list."""
205        return [i for i, bit in enumerate(vector) if bit == 1]
206
207    # After encode we may need a check_parameters method since most of the encoders have this
208    def check_parameters(self, parameters: "ScalarEncoderParameters"):
209        """
210        Check parameters method is used to verify that the correct parameters were entered
211        and reject the user when they are not.
212
213        Description: This changes and transforms the input that the user has with the parameters
214        dataclass. There are many aspects such as the active bit and sparsity being mutually exclusive
215        and the size, radius, resolution, and category also being muturally exclusive with each other.
216        The user will have an assert that rejects when these are violated.
217        """
218        if not parameters.minimum <= parameters.maximum:
219            raise ValueError("The minimum is not smaller than the maximum.")
220        num_active_args = sum([parameters.active_bits > 0, parameters.sparsity > 0])
221        if num_active_args == 0:
222            raise ValueError("Missing argument, need one of: 'active_bits', 'sparsity'")
223        if num_active_args != 1:
224            raise ValueError("Specified both: 'active_bits', 'sparsity'. Specify only one of them.")
225        num_size_args = sum(
226            [
227                parameters.radius > 0,
228                parameters.category,
229                parameters.resolution > 0,
230            ]
231        )
232        if num_size_args == 0:
233            raise ValueError("Missing argument, need one of: 'radius', 'resolution', 'category'.")
234        if num_size_args != 1:
235            raise ValueError(
236                "Too many arguments specified: 'radius', 'resolution', 'category'. Choose only one of them."
237            )
238        if parameters.periodic:
239            if parameters.clip_input:
240                raise ValueError("Will not clip periodic inputs.  Caller must apply modulus.")
241        if parameters.category:
242            if parameters.clip_input:
243                raise ValueError("Incompatible arguments: category & clip_input.")
244            if parameters.periodic:
245                raise ValueError("Incompatible arguments: category & periodic.")
246            if not parameters.minimum == float(int(parameters.minimum)):
247                raise ValueError(
248                    "Minimum input value of category encoder must be an unsigned integer!"
249                )
250            if not parameters.maximum == float(int(parameters.maximum)):
251                raise ValueError(
252                    "Maximum input value of category encoder must be an unsigned integer!"
253                )
254
255        args = parameters
256        if args.category:
257            args.radius = 1.0
258        if args.sparsity:
259            if args.sparsity <= 0.0 or args.sparsity >= 1.0:
260                raise ValueError("Sparsity should be between 0.0 and 1.0.")
261            if not args.size > 0:
262                raise ValueError("Argument 'sparsity' requires that the 'size' also be given.")
263            args.active_bits = round(args.size * args.sparsity)
264            if not args.active_bits > 0:
265                raise ValueError("Sparsity and size must be given so that sparsity * size > 0!")
266        if args.periodic:
267            extent_width = args.maximum - args.minimum
268        else:
269            max_inclusive = math.nextafter(args.maximum, math.inf)
270            extent_width = max_inclusive - args.minimum
271        if args.size > 0:
272            if args.periodic:
273                args.resolution = extent_width / args.size
274            else:
275                n_buckets = args.size - (args.active_bits - 1)
276                args.resolution = extent_width / (n_buckets - 1)
277        else:
278            if args.radius > 0.0:
279                args.resolution = args.radius / args.active_bits
280
281            needed_bands = math.ceil(extent_width / args.resolution)
282            if args.periodic:
283                args.size = needed_bands
284            else:
285                args.size = needed_bands + (args.active_bits - 1)
286
287        # Sanity check the parameters.
288        if not args.size > 0:
289            raise ValueError("Sanity check for size failed, it was not greater than 0.")
290        if not args.active_bits > 0:
291            raise ValueError("Sanity check for active bits failed, it was not greater than 0.")
292        if not args.active_bits < args.size:
293            raise ValueError("Sanity check for active bits being less than size failed.")
294
295        args.radius = args.active_bits * args.resolution
296        if not args.radius > 0:
297            raise ValueError("Sanity check failed for radius, it was not greater than 0.")
298
299        args.sparsity = args.active_bits / float(args.size)
300        if not args.sparsity > 0:
301            raise ValueError("Sanity check failed for sparsity, it was not greater than 0.")
302
303        return args

Encoder for numeric values using contiguous blocks of active bits.

The ScalarEncoder converts a numeric value into a sparse distributed representation where active bits form a contiguous block. The position of this block varies smoothly with the input value, ensuring semantic similarity - similar inputs produce overlapping representations.

Args: parameters: Configuration for scalar encoding behavior.

ScalarEncoder( parameters: ScalarEncoderParameters) 
38    def __init__(
39        self,
40        parameters: ScalarEncoderParameters,
41    ):
42        self._parameters = copy.deepcopy(parameters)
43        self._parameters = self.check_parameters(self._parameters)
44
45        self._minimum = self._parameters.minimum
46        self._maximum = self._parameters.maximum
47        self._clip_input = self._parameters.clip_input
48        self._periodic = self._parameters.periodic
49        self._category = self._parameters.category
50        self._active_bits = self._parameters.active_bits
51        self._sparsity = self._parameters.sparsity
52        self._size = self._parameters.size
53        self._radius = self._parameters.radius
54        self._resolution = self._parameters.resolution
55        self._encoding_cache: dict[float, list[int]] = {}
56        self.logger = get_logger(self)
57        super().__init__(self._size)
logger
size: int 
67    @property
68    def size(self) -> int:
69        return self._size

Get the total number of bits in the output SDR.

Returns: Total size of the SDR.

def register_encoding(self, input_value: float, encoded: list[int] | None = None) -> list[int]: 
86    def register_encoding(self, input_value: float, encoded: list[int] | None = None) -> list[int]:
87        """Caches an encoding so decode_closest can compare against it."""
88        vector = encoded if encoded is not None else self._compute_encoding(input_value)
89        if len(vector) != self.size:
90            raise ValueError("Stored encoding must be the same length as encoder size")
91        self._encoding_cache[input_value] = vector
92        return vector

Caches an encoding so decode_closest can compare against it.

def clear_registered_encodings(self) -> None: 
94    def clear_registered_encodings(self) -> None:
95        """Clears cached encodings used for nearest-neighbor decoding."""
96        self._encoding_cache.clear()

Clears cached encodings used for nearest-neighbor decoding.

@override
def encode(self, input_value: Any) -> list[int]: 
142    @override
143    def encode(self, input_value: Any) -> list[int]:
144        """Encode the input value into a binary vector."""
145        if type(input_value) is not int and type(input_value) is not float:
146            raise ValueError("A scalar encoder can only encode floats or ints.")
147        self.register_encoding(input_value)
148        self.logger.info("Scalar encoded value: %s", input_value)
149        return self._compute_encoding(input_value)

Encode the input value into a binary vector.

@override
def decode( self, encoded: list[int], candidates: Optional[Iterable[float]] = None) -> tuple[float | None, float]: 
151    @override
152    def decode(
153        self, encoded: list[int], candidates: Iterable[float] | None = None
154    ) -> tuple[float | None, float]:
155        """Returns the value whose encoding overlaps the most with the provided SDR."""
156        if len(encoded) != self.size:
157            raise ValueError(
158                f"Encoded input size ({len(encoded)}) does not match encoder size ({self.size})"
159            )
160
161        search_values = (
162            list(candidates) if candidates is not None else list(self._encoding_cache.keys())
163        )
164        if not search_values:
165            raise ValueError("No candidate encodings available for decoding")
166
167        best_value: float | None = None
168        best_overlap = -1
169
170        for candidate in search_values:
171            candidate_encoding = self._encoding_cache.get(candidate)
172            if candidate_encoding is None:
173                candidate_encoding = self.register_encoding(candidate)
174            overlap = self._overlap(encoded, candidate_encoding)
175            if overlap > best_overlap:
176                best_overlap = overlap
177                best_value = candidate
178
179        confidence = (
180            best_overlap / self._active_bits if best_overlap >= 0 and self._active_bits else 0.0
181        )
182        self.logger.info(
183            "Scalar decoded SDR into value: %s, with confidence: %s", best_value, confidence
184        )
185        return best_value, confidence

Returns the value whose encoding overlaps the most with the provided SDR.

def sparsify(self, vector: list[int]) -> list[int]: 
203    def sparsify(self, vector: list[int]) -> list[int]:
204        """Converts a sparse activity vector to a activation list."""
205        return [i for i, bit in enumerate(vector) if bit == 1]

Converts a sparse activity vector to a activation list.

def check_parameters( self, parameters: ScalarEncoderParameters): 
208    def check_parameters(self, parameters: "ScalarEncoderParameters"):
209        """
210        Check parameters method is used to verify that the correct parameters were entered
211        and reject the user when they are not.
212
213        Description: This changes and transforms the input that the user has with the parameters
214        dataclass. There are many aspects such as the active bit and sparsity being mutually exclusive
215        and the size, radius, resolution, and category also being muturally exclusive with each other.
216        The user will have an assert that rejects when these are violated.
217        """
218        if not parameters.minimum <= parameters.maximum:
219            raise ValueError("The minimum is not smaller than the maximum.")
220        num_active_args = sum([parameters.active_bits > 0, parameters.sparsity > 0])
221        if num_active_args == 0:
222            raise ValueError("Missing argument, need one of: 'active_bits', 'sparsity'")
223        if num_active_args != 1:
224            raise ValueError("Specified both: 'active_bits', 'sparsity'. Specify only one of them.")
225        num_size_args = sum(
226            [
227                parameters.radius > 0,
228                parameters.category,
229                parameters.resolution > 0,
230            ]
231        )
232        if num_size_args == 0:
233            raise ValueError("Missing argument, need one of: 'radius', 'resolution', 'category'.")
234        if num_size_args != 1:
235            raise ValueError(
236                "Too many arguments specified: 'radius', 'resolution', 'category'. Choose only one of them."
237            )
238        if parameters.periodic:
239            if parameters.clip_input:
240                raise ValueError("Will not clip periodic inputs.  Caller must apply modulus.")
241        if parameters.category:
242            if parameters.clip_input:
243                raise ValueError("Incompatible arguments: category & clip_input.")
244            if parameters.periodic:
245                raise ValueError("Incompatible arguments: category & periodic.")
246            if not parameters.minimum == float(int(parameters.minimum)):
247                raise ValueError(
248                    "Minimum input value of category encoder must be an unsigned integer!"
249                )
250            if not parameters.maximum == float(int(parameters.maximum)):
251                raise ValueError(
252                    "Maximum input value of category encoder must be an unsigned integer!"
253                )
254
255        args = parameters
256        if args.category:
257            args.radius = 1.0
258        if args.sparsity:
259            if args.sparsity <= 0.0 or args.sparsity >= 1.0:
260                raise ValueError("Sparsity should be between 0.0 and 1.0.")
261            if not args.size > 0:
262                raise ValueError("Argument 'sparsity' requires that the 'size' also be given.")
263            args.active_bits = round(args.size * args.sparsity)
264            if not args.active_bits > 0:
265                raise ValueError("Sparsity and size must be given so that sparsity * size > 0!")
266        if args.periodic:
267            extent_width = args.maximum - args.minimum
268        else:
269            max_inclusive = math.nextafter(args.maximum, math.inf)
270            extent_width = max_inclusive - args.minimum
271        if args.size > 0:
272            if args.periodic:
273                args.resolution = extent_width / args.size
274            else:
275                n_buckets = args.size - (args.active_bits - 1)
276                args.resolution = extent_width / (n_buckets - 1)
277        else:
278            if args.radius > 0.0:
279                args.resolution = args.radius / args.active_bits
280
281            needed_bands = math.ceil(extent_width / args.resolution)
282            if args.periodic:
283                args.size = needed_bands
284            else:
285                args.size = needed_bands + (args.active_bits - 1)
286
287        # Sanity check the parameters.
288        if not args.size > 0:
289            raise ValueError("Sanity check for size failed, it was not greater than 0.")
290        if not args.active_bits > 0:
291            raise ValueError("Sanity check for active bits failed, it was not greater than 0.")
292        if not args.active_bits < args.size:
293            raise ValueError("Sanity check for active bits being less than size failed.")
294
295        args.radius = args.active_bits * args.resolution
296        if not args.radius > 0:
297            raise ValueError("Sanity check failed for radius, it was not greater than 0.")
298
299        args.sparsity = args.active_bits / float(args.size)
300        if not args.sparsity > 0:
301            raise ValueError("Sanity check failed for sparsity, it was not greater than 0.")
302
303        return args

Check parameters method is used to verify that the correct parameters were entered and reject the user when they are not.

Description: This changes and transforms the input that the user has with the parameters dataclass. There are many aspects such as the active bit and sparsity being mutually exclusive and the size, radius, resolution, and category also being muturally exclusive with each other. The user will have an assert that rejects when these are violated.

@dataclass
class ScalarEncoderParameters: 
306@dataclass
307class ScalarEncoderParameters:
308    """Configuration parameters for :class:`ScalarEncoder`."""
309
310    minimum: int = 0
311    """Min and Max
312     * Members "minimum" and "maximum" define the range of the input signal.
313     * These endpoints are inclusive.
314     */"""
315    maximum: int = 1000
316    """Min and Max
317     * Members "minimum" and "maximum" define the range of the input signal.
318     * These endpoints are inclusive.
319     */"""
320
321    clip_input: bool = False
322    """Whether to clip inputs outside the min/max range.
323       /**
324     * Member "clipInput" determines whether to allow input values outside the
325     * range [minimum, maximum].
326     * If true, the input will be clipped into the range [minimum, maximum].
327     * If false, inputs outside of the range will raise an error.
328     */"""
329
330    periodic: bool = False
331    """Whether the encoder is periodic (circular) or not.
332    /**
333     * Member "periodic" controls what happens near the edges of the input
334     * range.
335     *
336     * If true, then the minimum & maximum input values are the same and the
337     * first and last bits of the output SDR are adjacent.  The contiguous
338     * block of 1's wraps around the end back to the beginning.
339     *
340     * If false, then minimum & maximum input values are the endpoints of the
341     * input range, are not adjacent, and activity does not wrap around.
342     */
343    """
344
345    category: bool = False
346    """Whether the encoder is a category encoder.
347    /**
348     * Member "category" means that the inputs are enumerated categories.
349     * If true then this encoder will only encode unsigned integers, and all
350     * inputs will have unique / non-overlapping representations.
351     */
352    """
353    active_bits: int = 40
354    """Number of active bits in the output SDR.
355     * Member "activeBits" is the number of true bits in the encoded output SDR.
356     * The output encodings will have a contiguous block of this many 1's.
357    """
358    sparsity: float = 0.0
359    """Sparsity level -- % of active bits.
360    * Member "sparsity" is an alternative way to specify the member "activeBits".
361    * Sparsity requires that the size to also be specified.
362    * Specify only one of: activeBits or sparsity.
363    """
364    size: int = 2048
365    """Total number of bits in the output SDR.
366     /**
367     * Member "size" is the total number of bits in the encoded output SDR.
368     */
369    """
370    radius: float = 1.0
371    """Approximate input range (width) covered by the active bits.
372    /**
373     * Member "radius" Two inputs separated by more than the radius have
374     * non-overlapping representations. Two inputs separated by less than the
375     * radius will in general overlap in at least some of their bits. You can
376     * think of this as the radius of the input.
377     */
378    """
379    resolution: float = 0.0
380    """The smallest difference between two inputs that produces different outputs.
381      /**
382     * Member "resolution" Two inputs separated by greater than, or equal to the
383     * resolution are guaranteed to have different representations.
384     */"""
385    encoder_class = ScalarEncoder

Configuration parameters for ScalarEncoder.

ScalarEncoderParameters( minimum: int = 0, maximum: int = 1000, clip_input: bool = False, periodic: bool = False, category: bool = False, active_bits: int = 40, sparsity: float = 0.0, size: int = 2048, radius: float = 1.0, resolution: float = 0.0)
minimum: int = 0

Min and Max

  • Members "minimum" and "maximum" define the range of the input signal.
  • These endpoints are inclusive. */
maximum: int = 1000

Min and Max

  • Members "minimum" and "maximum" define the range of the input signal.
  • These endpoints are inclusive. */
clip_input: bool = False

Whether to clip inputs outside the min/max range. /**

  • Member "clipInput" determines whether to allow input values outside the
  • range [minimum, maximum].
  • If true, the input will be clipped into the range [minimum, maximum].
  • If false, inputs outside of the range will raise an error. */
periodic: bool = False

Whether the encoder is periodic (circular) or not. /**

  • Member "periodic" controls what happens near the edges of the input
  • range. *
  • If true, then the minimum & maximum input values are the same and the
  • first and last bits of the output SDR are adjacent. The contiguous
  • block of 1's wraps around the end back to the beginning. *
  • If false, then minimum & maximum input values are the endpoints of the
  • input range, are not adjacent, and activity does not wrap around. */
category: bool = False

Whether the encoder is a category encoder. /**

  • Member "category" means that the inputs are enumerated categories.
  • If true then this encoder will only encode unsigned integers, and all
  • inputs will have unique / non-overlapping representations. */
active_bits: int = 40

Number of active bits in the output SDR.

  • Member "activeBits" is the number of true bits in the encoded output SDR.
  • The output encodings will have a contiguous block of this many 1's.
sparsity: float = 0.0

Sparsity level -- % of active bits.

  • Member "sparsity" is an alternative way to specify the member "activeBits".
  • Sparsity requires that the size to also be specified.
  • Specify only one of: activeBits or sparsity.
size: int = 2048

Total number of bits in the output SDR. /**

  • Member "size" is the total number of bits in the encoded output SDR. */
radius: float = 1.0

Approximate input range (width) covered by the active bits. /**

  • Member "radius" Two inputs separated by more than the radius have
  • non-overlapping representations. Two inputs separated by less than the
  • radius will in general overlap in at least some of their bits. You can
  • think of this as the radius of the input. */
resolution: float = 0.0

The smallest difference between two inputs that produces different outputs. /**

  • Member "resolution" Two inputs separated by greater than, or equal to the
  • resolution are guaranteed to have different representations. */
encoder_class = <class 'ScalarEncoder'>
class FourierEncoder(psu_capstone.encoder_layer.BaseEncoder[numpy.ndarray], list[int]): 
 32class FourierEncoder(BaseEncoder[np.ndarray], list[int]):
 33    """Encoder that uses Fourier Transform on time data. Build RDSE for each frequency component. Assume that time domain is in seconds.
 34
 35    Args:
 36        parameters: Fourier encoder parameters. Defaults to FourierEncoderParameters().
 37    """
 38
 39    def __init__(self, parameters: FourierEncoderParameters):
 40
 41        if parameters is None:
 42            parameters = FourierEncoderParameters()
 43
 44        # set the size of the base encoder
 45        super().__init__(parameters.size)
 46
 47        self._params = copy.deepcopy(parameters)
 48        """Fourier encoder local copy of passed parameters."""
 49        self._sdrs: list[list[int]] = []
 50        """List to hold the SDRs for each frequency range."""
 51        # encoder params
 52        self._sensitivity = self._params.sensitivity_threshold
 53        """Magnitude threshold for considering a frequency component as a peak."""
 54        self._frequency_ranges = self._params.frequency_ranges
 55        """List of frequency ranges to find."""
 56        self._size = self._params.size  # default to 2048
 57        """Size of the encoder."""
 58        self._total_active_bits = self._params.total_active_bits  # default to 40
 59        """Total number of active bits in the encoder output SDR."""
 60        self._active_bits_in_ranges = self._params.active_bits_in_ranges
 61        """The number of active bits per frequency range in the encoder output SDR."""
 62        self._sparsity_in_ranges = self._params.sparsity_in_ranges
 63        """The sparsity per frequency range in the encoder output SDR."""
 64        self._resolutions_in_ranges = self._params.resolutions_in_ranges
 65        """The resolution per frequency range in the encoder output SDR."""
 66        self._seed = self._params.seed  # default to 32
 67        """Random seed for reproducibility."""
 68        self._bucket_sizes: list[int] = []
 69        """The frequency intervals."""
 70
 71        # time domain params
 72        self._start_time = self._params.start_time  # default to 0.0
 73        """Start time of the time interval."""
 74        self._stop_time = self._params.stop_time  # default to 1.0
 75        """Stop time of the time interval."""
 76        self._period_size = self._stop_time - self._start_time
 77        """Total time period size in seconds."""
 78        self._total_samples = self._params.total_samples  # default to 256
 79        """Total number of samples in the period."""
 80        self._time_step = self._period_size / self._total_samples
 81        """Time step between samples."""
 82        self._sample_rate = float(1 / self._time_step)  # samples per second
 83        """Sample rate in Hz."""
 84
 85        self._validate_params(self._params)
 86        self._calc_bucket_sizes()
 87
 88    @property
 89    def sdrs(self) -> list[list[int]]:
 90        """Get the list of SDRs for each frequency range."""
 91        return self._sdrs
 92
 93    def _normalize(self, input: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
 94        """Normalize the FFT array to a unit vector."""
 95
 96        # Calculate the normal vector norm (L2 norm)
 97        norm = np.linalg.norm(input)
 98
 99        unit_vector = input / norm if norm != 0 else input
100
101        return unit_vector
102
103    def _trim(self, input_data: np.ndarray | list[float]) -> np.ndarray:
104        """Trim input array into a power of 2 size"""
105
106        # verify sizes of input data
107        self._total_samples = len(input_data)
108        self._time_step = self._period_size / self._total_samples
109        self._sample_rate = float(1 / self._time_step)  # samples per second
110
111        if isinstance(input_data, np.ndarray):
112            input_data = input_data.astype(float, copy=False)
113
114        else:
115            input_data = np.asarray(input_data, dtype=float)
116
117        # Always operate on a flattened 1D array so FFT math works correctly
118        input_data = np.asarray(input_data, dtype=float).reshape(-1)
119
120        total_samples = len(input_data)
121
122        if total_samples & (total_samples - 1) != 0:
123
124            target_size = 1 << (total_samples - 1).bit_length()
125            padding = target_size - total_samples
126            input_data = np.pad(input_data, (0, padding), mode="constant")
127
128            logger.info(
129                (
130                    "FFT input adjusted:\n"
131                    f"  • Original samples: {total_samples}\n"
132                    f"  • Target samples:   {target_size}\n"
133                    f"  • Zero padding:     {padding}\n"
134                )
135            )
136
137        return copy.deepcopy(input_data)
138
139    def _calc_bucket_sizes(self) -> None:
140        """Calculate the bucket sizes for each frequency range."""
141
142        for i in range(len(self._frequency_ranges)):
143
144            freq_range = self._frequency_ranges[i]
145            start_freq = freq_range[0]
146            stop_freq = freq_range[1]
147
148            bucket_size = stop_freq - start_freq
149
150            self._bucket_sizes.append(bucket_size)
151
152    def _find_num_peaks(self, freq_data: np.ndarray) -> int:
153        """Find the number of peaks in the given frequency data."""
154
155        num_peaks = 0
156        threshold = self._sensitivity
157
158        for i in range(len(freq_data)):
159            if freq_data[i] > threshold:
160                num_peaks += 1
161        return num_peaks
162
163    @override
164    def encode(self, input_value: Any) -> list[int]:
165        """Transform the input signal via FFT and populate the provided SDR.
166
167        Encodes a single frequency peak into a SDR as list[int].
168
169        Args:
170            input_value: np.ndarray | list[float]: The input time-domain signal to be encoded.
171
172        Returns:
173            list[int]: List of active bit indices in the encoded SDR.
174
175        Raises:
176
177            ValueError: If the input signal is not a 1D array or if the encoder parameters are invalid.
178
179        """
180
181        # validate parameters
182
183        self._validate_params(self._params)
184
185        if not isinstance(input_value, (np.ndarray, list)):
186            raise ValueError("Input value must be a numpy array or a list of floats.")
187
188        #  trim input data into a power of 2 size and reset internal params
189        input_value = self._trim(cast(np.ndarray, input_value))
190
191        # subtract mean to center the signal around zero
192        input_value = input_value - np.mean(input_value)
193
194        samples = self._total_samples
195        time_step = self._time_step
196        freq_ranges = self._frequency_ranges
197        threshold = self._sensitivity
198
199        dense_bits: list[int] = []
200
201        #  list to hold values if we want to perform a mean later
202        magnitudes = []
203
204        #  perform FFT on input time data and normalize
205        freq_data = cast(np.ndarray, fft(input_value))
206
207        # Remove the DC bucket so normalization is not dominated by zero Hz energy
208        if freq_data.size > 0:
209            freq_data[0] = 0
210        freq_data = self._normalize(freq_data)
211
212        # Nyquist frequency limit and store freq buckets as indexes
213        freq_data = freq_data[: samples // 2]
214
215        all_freqs = np.real(np.abs(freq_data))
216        all_peaks = self._find_num_peaks(all_freqs)
217        logger.info(f"Total peaks found in signal: {all_peaks}")
218
219        # LOOP THROUGH FREQUENCY RANGES !!
220        print("Looping through frequency ranges:")
221        for freq_range in freq_ranges:
222            # reset total size
223            size = self._size
224
225            current_dense_bits: list[int] = []
226
227            start_freq = freq_range[0]
228            stop_freq = freq_range[1]
229
230            if stop_freq > (self._sample_rate / 2):
231                logger.warning(
232                    f"Frequency range end {stop_freq} exceeds Nyquist frequency {self._sample_rate / 2} Hz. Adjusting to Nyquist limit."
233                )
234                stop_freq = int(self._sample_rate // 2)
235
236            freq_buckets = fftfreq(samples, time_step)[start_freq:stop_freq]
237
238            # slice freq data to current frequency range  :: assuming freq ranges are in Hz
239            freq_slice = freq_data[start_freq:stop_freq]
240            freq_slice = np.real(np.abs(freq_slice))
241
242            # find peak frequency in the current interval
243            peak_index = np.argmax(np.abs(freq_slice))
244            if peak_index == 0:
245                logger.warning(f"!!-ZERO Peak Found in range--!! {freq_range}.")
246
247            if freq_slice[peak_index] < threshold:
248                logger.info(f"No significant peaks found in range {freq_range}.")
249
250                continue
251
252            else:
253                logger.info(
254                    f"FFT Peak Frequency in range {freq_range}: {float(freq_buckets[peak_index])} Hz with magnitude {freq_slice[peak_index]}"
255                )
256
257            # current resolution for this frequency range
258            current_res = self._resolutions_in_ranges[self._frequency_ranges.index(freq_range)]
259            current_interval_size = self._bucket_sizes[self._frequency_ranges.index(freq_range)]
260            current_sparsity = self._sparsity_in_ranges[self._frequency_ranges.index(freq_range)]
261
262            current_interval_size = min(current_interval_size, len(freq_slice))
263
264            num_peaks = self._find_num_peaks(freq_slice)
265            logger.info(f"Number of peaks found in range {freq_range}: {num_peaks}")
266
267            size = (
268                size // (num_peaks + 1) if num_peaks >= 1 else size
269            )  # take the floor of the division to ensure size is an integer
270
271            if size % 2 != 0:
272                size += 1  # ensure size is even for FFT symmetry
273
274            # build each encoder for frequency and magnitude :: thank you GC
275            freq_encoder = RandomDistributedScalarEncoder(
276                RDSEParameters(
277                    size=size,
278                    sparsity=current_sparsity,  # sparisity requested by user
279                    active_bits=0,
280                    resolution=current_res,
281                )
282            )
283
284            # encode the frequency range slice
285            for f in range(current_interval_size):
286
287                # find frequencies that contributed to the fft output by magnitude threshold
288                if freq_slice[f] < threshold:
289
290                    continue
291
292                else:
293
294                    freq_value = float(f + start_freq)
295
296                    sdr_freq = freq_encoder.encode(freq_value)
297                    magnitudes.append(freq_slice[f])
298
299                    logger.info(
300                        f"Encoding frequency bucket: {f + start_freq} with magnitude: {freq_slice[f]}"
301                    )
302                    current_dense_bits.extend(sdr_freq)
303
304            # END of INNER for loop :: freq intervals
305
306            #
307
308            magnitude_encoder = RandomDistributedScalarEncoder(
309                RDSEParameters(
310                    size=size,
311                    sparsity=current_sparsity,
312                    active_bits=0,
313                    resolution=time_step,
314                )
315            )
316
317            magnitude = float(np.mean(magnitudes))
318
319            print(f"Mean magnitude: {magnitude}")
320
321            sdr_magnitude = magnitude_encoder.encode(magnitude)
322
323            current_dense_bits.extend(sdr_magnitude)
324
325            if len(current_dense_bits) > self._size:
326                logger.warning(
327                    f"Encoded SDR size {len(current_dense_bits)} exceeds specified encoder size {self._size}. Consider increasing encoder size or adjusting sparsity/resolution parameters."
328                )
329                current_dense_bits = current_dense_bits[: self._size]
330
331            elif len(current_dense_bits) < self._size:
332                # pad with zeros if we have less bits than the encoder size
333                current_dense_bits.extend([0] * (self._size - len(current_dense_bits)))
334            dense_bits.extend(current_dense_bits)
335
336        # END of OUTER for loop
337        if len(dense_bits) > self.size:
338            logger.warning(
339                f"Total encoded SDR size {len(dense_bits)} exceeds specified encoder size {self.size}. Consider increasing encoder size or adjusting sparsity/resolution parameters."
340            )
341
342            number_of_sdrs = len(dense_bits) // self.size
343
344            dense_list = [
345                dense_bits[i * self.size : (i + 1) * self.size] for i in range(number_of_sdrs)
346            ]
347
348            self._sdrs = dense_list
349        return dense_bits
350        # END def encode
351
352    @override
353    def decode(
354        self, encoded: list[int], candidates: Iterable[float] | None = None
355    ) -> dict[str, list[tuple[tuple[int, int], float | None, float]] | tuple[float | None, float]]:
356        """Decode a combined Fourier SDR into per-range frequency estimates and magnitude."""
357        if len(encoded) != self.size:
358            raise ValueError(
359                f"Encoded input size ({len(encoded)}) does not match encoder size ({self.size})"
360            )
361
362        results: dict[
363            str, list[tuple[tuple[int, int], float | None, float]] | tuple[float | None, float]
364        ] = {"frequencies": []}
365
366        for range_index, freq_range in enumerate(self._frequency_ranges):
367            start_freq, stop_freq = freq_range
368            current_res = self._resolutions_in_ranges[range_index]
369            current_sparsity = self._sparsity_in_ranges[range_index]
370
371            freq_encoder = RandomDistributedScalarEncoder(
372                RDSEParameters(
373                    size=self._size,
374                    sparsity=current_sparsity,
375                    active_bits=0,
376                    resolution=current_res,
377                )
378            )
379
380            if candidates is not None:
381                range_candidates = [
382                    candidate for candidate in candidates if start_freq <= candidate < stop_freq
383                ]
384                if not range_candidates:
385                    range_candidates = list(range(start_freq, stop_freq))
386            else:
387                range_candidates = list(range(start_freq, stop_freq))
388            decoded_value, confidence = freq_encoder.decode(encoded, range_candidates)
389            results["frequencies"].append((freq_range, decoded_value, confidence))
390
391        magnitude_candidates: list[float] = []
392        if self._time_step > 0:
393            magnitude_candidates = list(
394                np.arange(0.0, 1.0 + self._time_step, self._time_step, dtype=float)
395            )
396
397        if magnitude_candidates:
398            magnitude_encoder = RandomDistributedScalarEncoder(
399                RDSEParameters(
400                    size=self._size,
401                    sparsity=current_sparsity,
402                    active_bits=0,
403                    resolution=current_res,
404                )
405            )
406            results["magnitude"] = magnitude_encoder.decode(encoded, magnitude_candidates)
407
408        return results
409
410    def _validate_params(self, parameters: FourierEncoderParameters) -> None:
411        """Check if the provided parameters are valid for the Fourier encoder.
412
413        Args:
414            parameters: The Fourier encoder parameters to check.
415
416        Raises:
417            ValueError: If parameters are invalid or inconsistent.
418        """
419
420        if parameters is None:
421            raise ValueError("FourierEncoderParameters cannot be None.")
422
423        params = copy.deepcopy(parameters)
424
425        if params.size <= 0:
426            raise ValueError("Encoder size must be greater than zero.")
427        if params.total_samples <= 0:
428            raise ValueError("total_samples must be greater than zero.")
429        if params.stop_time <= params.start_time:
430            raise ValueError("stop_time must be greater than start_time.")
431        if params.seed < 0:
432            raise ValueError("seed must be non-negative.")
433        if params.sensitivity_threshold < 0:
434            raise ValueError("sensitivity_threshold must be non-negative.")
435
436        num_ranges = len(params.frequency_ranges)
437        if num_ranges == 0:
438            raise ValueError("At least one frequency range must be provided.")
439
440        # Active bits and sparsity are mutually exclusive controls.
441        if params.active_bits_in_ranges and params.sparsity_in_ranges:
442            raise ValueError(
443                "Cannot specify both active_bits_in_ranges and sparsity_in_ranges. Choose one."
444            )
445
446        # Normalize per-range resolutions: allow a single global value.
447        if len(params.resolutions_in_ranges) == 0:
448            raise ValueError("resolutions_in_ranges must contain at least one value.")
449        if len(params.resolutions_in_ranges) == 1 and num_ranges > 1:
450            params.resolutions_in_ranges = [params.resolutions_in_ranges[0]] * num_ranges
451        elif len(params.resolutions_in_ranges) != num_ranges:
452            raise ValueError(
453                "resolutions_in_ranges must either have one global value or match frequency_ranges length."
454            )
455
456        if any(resolution <= 0 for resolution in params.resolutions_in_ranges):
457            raise ValueError("All resolutions_in_ranges values must be greater than zero.")
458
459        # Normalize sparsity/active_bits to per-range values.
460        if params.sparsity_in_ranges:
461            if len(params.sparsity_in_ranges) == 1 and num_ranges > 1:
462                params.sparsity_in_ranges = [params.sparsity_in_ranges[0]] * num_ranges
463            elif len(params.sparsity_in_ranges) != num_ranges:
464                raise ValueError(
465                    "sparsity_in_ranges must either have one global value or match frequency_ranges length."
466                )
467
468            if any(sparsity <= 0 or sparsity > 1 for sparsity in params.sparsity_in_ranges):
469                raise ValueError("Each sparsity value must be in the interval (0, 1].")
470
471        elif params.active_bits_in_ranges:
472            if len(params.active_bits_in_ranges) == 1 and num_ranges > 1:
473                params.active_bits_in_ranges = [params.active_bits_in_ranges[0]] * num_ranges
474            elif len(params.active_bits_in_ranges) != num_ranges:
475                raise ValueError(
476                    "active_bits_in_ranges must either have one global value or match frequency_ranges length."
477                )
478
479            if any(active_bits <= 0 for active_bits in params.active_bits_in_ranges):
480                raise ValueError("All active_bits_in_ranges values must be greater than zero.")
481            if any(active_bits > params.size for active_bits in params.active_bits_in_ranges):
482                raise ValueError("active_bits_in_ranges values cannot exceed encoder size.")
483
484            params.sparsity_in_ranges = [
485                active_bits / params.size for active_bits in params.active_bits_in_ranges
486            ]
487
488        else:
489            if params.total_active_bits > 0:
490                if params.total_active_bits > params.size:
491                    raise ValueError("total_active_bits cannot exceed encoder size.")
492                params.sparsity_in_ranges = [params.total_active_bits / params.size] * num_ranges
493            else:
494                raise ValueError(
495                    "Provide sparsity_in_ranges, active_bits_in_ranges, or total_active_bits > 0."
496                )
497
498        sample_rate = float(params.total_samples / (params.stop_time - params.start_time))
499        nyquist = sample_rate / 2
500
501        normalized_ranges = sorted(params.frequency_ranges, key=lambda value: value[0])
502        previous_end: int | None = None
503
504        for start_freq, stop_freq in normalized_ranges:
505            if start_freq < 0 or stop_freq < 0:
506                raise ValueError("Frequency ranges must be non-negative.")
507            if start_freq >= stop_freq:
508                raise ValueError("Frequency range start must be less than end.")
509            if stop_freq > nyquist:
510                raise ValueError(
511                    f"Frequency range end {stop_freq} exceeds Nyquist frequency {nyquist} Hz."
512                )
513
514            if previous_end is not None and start_freq < previous_end:
515                raise ValueError("Frequency ranges must not overlap.")
516            previous_end = stop_freq
517
518        predicted_sdr_size = params.size * num_ranges
519        if predicted_sdr_size % params.size != 0:
520            raise ValueError(
521                "Predicted SDR shape is inconsistent with encoder size and frequency range count."
522            )
523
524        # Persist normalized values so encode/decode can rely on validated per-range lists.
525        parameters.resolutions_in_ranges = params.resolutions_in_ranges
526        parameters.sparsity_in_ranges = params.sparsity_in_ranges
527        parameters.active_bits_in_ranges = params.active_bits_in_ranges

Encoder that uses Fourier Transform on time data. Build RDSE for each frequency component. Assume that time domain is in seconds.

Args: parameters: Fourier encoder parameters. Defaults to FourierEncoderParameters().

FourierEncoder( parameters: FourierEncoderParameters) 
39    def __init__(self, parameters: FourierEncoderParameters):
40
41        if parameters is None:
42            parameters = FourierEncoderParameters()
43
44        # set the size of the base encoder
45        super().__init__(parameters.size)
46
47        self._params = copy.deepcopy(parameters)
48        """Fourier encoder local copy of passed parameters."""
49        self._sdrs: list[list[int]] = []
50        """List to hold the SDRs for each frequency range."""
51        # encoder params
52        self._sensitivity = self._params.sensitivity_threshold
