53        """Magnitude threshold for considering a frequency component as a peak."""
54        self._frequency_ranges = self._params.frequency_ranges
55        """List of frequency ranges to find."""
56        self._size = self._params.size  # default to 2048
57        """Size of the encoder."""
58        self._total_active_bits = self._params.total_active_bits  # default to 40
59        """Total number of active bits in the encoder output SDR."""
60        self._active_bits_in_ranges = self._params.active_bits_in_ranges
61        """The number of active bits per frequency range in the encoder output SDR."""
62        self._sparsity_in_ranges = self._params.sparsity_in_ranges
63        """The sparsity per frequency range in the encoder output SDR."""
64        self._resolutions_in_ranges = self._params.resolutions_in_ranges
65        """The resolution per frequency range in the encoder output SDR."""
66        self._seed = self._params.seed  # default to 32
67        """Random seed for reproducibility."""
68        self._bucket_sizes: list[int] = []
69        """The frequency intervals."""
70
71        # time domain params
72        self._start_time = self._params.start_time  # default to 0.0
73        """Start time of the time interval."""
74        self._stop_time = self._params.stop_time  # default to 1.0
75        """Stop time of the time interval."""
76        self._period_size = self._stop_time - self._start_time
77        """Total time period size in seconds."""
78        self._total_samples = self._params.total_samples  # default to 256
79        """Total number of samples in the period."""
80        self._time_step = self._period_size / self._total_samples
81        """Time step between samples."""
82        self._sample_rate = float(1 / self._time_step)  # samples per second
83        """Sample rate in Hz."""
84
85        self._validate_params(self._params)
86        self._calc_bucket_sizes()
sdrs: list[list[int]] 
88    @property
89    def sdrs(self) -> list[list[int]]:
90        """Get the list of SDRs for each frequency range."""
91        return self._sdrs

Get the list of SDRs for each frequency range.

@override
def encode(self, input_value: Any) -> list[int]: 
163    @override
164    def encode(self, input_value: Any) -> list[int]:
165        """Transform the input signal via FFT and populate the provided SDR.
166
167        Encodes a single frequency peak into a SDR as list[int].
168
169        Args:
170            input_value: np.ndarray | list[float]: The input time-domain signal to be encoded.
171
172        Returns:
173            list[int]: List of active bit indices in the encoded SDR.
174
175        Raises:
176
177            ValueError: If the input signal is not a 1D array or if the encoder parameters are invalid.
178
179        """
180
181        # validate parameters
182
183        self._validate_params(self._params)
184
185        if not isinstance(input_value, (np.ndarray, list)):
186            raise ValueError("Input value must be a numpy array or a list of floats.")
187
188        #  trim input data into a power of 2 size and reset internal params
189        input_value = self._trim(cast(np.ndarray, input_value))
190
191        # subtract mean to center the signal around zero
192        input_value = input_value - np.mean(input_value)
193
194        samples = self._total_samples
195        time_step = self._time_step
196        freq_ranges = self._frequency_ranges
197        threshold = self._sensitivity
198
199        dense_bits: list[int] = []
200
201        #  list to hold values if we want to perform a mean later
202        magnitudes = []
203
204        #  perform FFT on input time data and normalize
205        freq_data = cast(np.ndarray, fft(input_value))
206
207        # Remove the DC bucket so normalization is not dominated by zero Hz energy
208        if freq_data.size > 0:
209            freq_data[0] = 0
210        freq_data = self._normalize(freq_data)
211
212        # Nyquist frequency limit and store freq buckets as indexes
213        freq_data = freq_data[: samples // 2]
214
215        all_freqs = np.real(np.abs(freq_data))
216        all_peaks = self._find_num_peaks(all_freqs)
217        logger.info(f"Total peaks found in signal: {all_peaks}")
218
219        # LOOP THROUGH FREQUENCY RANGES !!
220        print("Looping through frequency ranges:")
221        for freq_range in freq_ranges:
222            # reset total size
223            size = self._size
224
225            current_dense_bits: list[int] = []
226
227            start_freq = freq_range[0]
228            stop_freq = freq_range[1]
229
230            if stop_freq > (self._sample_rate / 2):
231                logger.warning(
232                    f"Frequency range end {stop_freq} exceeds Nyquist frequency {self._sample_rate / 2} Hz. Adjusting to Nyquist limit."
233                )
234                stop_freq = int(self._sample_rate // 2)
235
236            freq_buckets = fftfreq(samples, time_step)[start_freq:stop_freq]
237
238            # slice freq data to current frequency range  :: assuming freq ranges are in Hz
239            freq_slice = freq_data[start_freq:stop_freq]
240            freq_slice = np.real(np.abs(freq_slice))
241
242            # find peak frequency in the current interval
243            peak_index = np.argmax(np.abs(freq_slice))
244            if peak_index == 0:
245                logger.warning(f"!!-ZERO Peak Found in range--!! {freq_range}.")
246
247            if freq_slice[peak_index] < threshold:
248                logger.info(f"No significant peaks found in range {freq_range}.")
249
250                continue
251
252            else:
253                logger.info(
254                    f"FFT Peak Frequency in range {freq_range}: {float(freq_buckets[peak_index])} Hz with magnitude {freq_slice[peak_index]}"
255                )
256
257            # current resolution for this frequency range
258            current_res = self._resolutions_in_ranges[self._frequency_ranges.index(freq_range)]
259            current_interval_size = self._bucket_sizes[self._frequency_ranges.index(freq_range)]
260            current_sparsity = self._sparsity_in_ranges[self._frequency_ranges.index(freq_range)]
261
262            current_interval_size = min(current_interval_size, len(freq_slice))
263
264            num_peaks = self._find_num_peaks(freq_slice)
265            logger.info(f"Number of peaks found in range {freq_range}: {num_peaks}")
266
267            size = (
268                size // (num_peaks + 1) if num_peaks >= 1 else size
269            )  # take the floor of the division to ensure size is an integer
270
271            if size % 2 != 0:
272                size += 1  # ensure size is even for FFT symmetry
273
274            # build each encoder for frequency and magnitude :: thank you GC
275            freq_encoder = RandomDistributedScalarEncoder(
276                RDSEParameters(
277                    size=size,
278                    sparsity=current_sparsity,  # sparisity requested by user
279                    active_bits=0,
280                    resolution=current_res,
281                )
282            )
283
284            # encode the frequency range slice
285            for f in range(current_interval_size):
286
287                # find frequencies that contributed to the fft output by magnitude threshold
288                if freq_slice[f] < threshold:
289
290                    continue
291
292                else:
293
294                    freq_value = float(f + start_freq)
295
296                    sdr_freq = freq_encoder.encode(freq_value)
297                    magnitudes.append(freq_slice[f])
298
299                    logger.info(
300                        f"Encoding frequency bucket: {f + start_freq} with magnitude: {freq_slice[f]}"
301                    )
302                    current_dense_bits.extend(sdr_freq)
303
304            # END of INNER for loop :: freq intervals
305
306            #
307
308            magnitude_encoder = RandomDistributedScalarEncoder(
309                RDSEParameters(
310                    size=size,
311                    sparsity=current_sparsity,
312                    active_bits=0,
313                    resolution=time_step,
314                )
315            )
316
317            magnitude = float(np.mean(magnitudes))
318
319            print(f"Mean magnitude: {magnitude}")
320
321            sdr_magnitude = magnitude_encoder.encode(magnitude)
322
323            current_dense_bits.extend(sdr_magnitude)
324
325            if len(current_dense_bits) > self._size:
326                logger.warning(
327                    f"Encoded SDR size {len(current_dense_bits)} exceeds specified encoder size {self._size}. Consider increasing encoder size or adjusting sparsity/resolution parameters."
328                )
329                current_dense_bits = current_dense_bits[: self._size]
330
331            elif len(current_dense_bits) < self._size:
332                # pad with zeros if we have less bits than the encoder size
333                current_dense_bits.extend([0] * (self._size - len(current_dense_bits)))
334            dense_bits.extend(current_dense_bits)
335
336        # END of OUTER for loop
337        if len(dense_bits) > self.size:
338            logger.warning(
339                f"Total encoded SDR size {len(dense_bits)} exceeds specified encoder size {self.size}. Consider increasing encoder size or adjusting sparsity/resolution parameters."
340            )
341
342            number_of_sdrs = len(dense_bits) // self.size
343
344            dense_list = [
345                dense_bits[i * self.size : (i + 1) * self.size] for i in range(number_of_sdrs)
346            ]
347
348            self._sdrs = dense_list
349        return dense_bits
350        # END def encode

Transform the input signal via FFT and populate the provided SDR.

Encodes a single frequency peak into a SDR as list[int].

Args: input_value: np.ndarray | list[float]: The input time-domain signal to be encoded.

Returns: list[int]: List of active bit indices in the encoded SDR.

Raises:

ValueError: If the input signal is not a 1D array or if the encoder parameters are invalid.
@override
def decode( self, encoded: list[int], candidates: Optional[Iterable[float]] = None) -> dict[str, list[tuple[tuple[int, int], float | None, float]] | tuple[float | None, float]]: 
352    @override
353    def decode(
354        self, encoded: list[int], candidates: Iterable[float] | None = None
355    ) -> dict[str, list[tuple[tuple[int, int], float | None, float]] | tuple[float | None, float]]:
356        """Decode a combined Fourier SDR into per-range frequency estimates and magnitude."""
357        if len(encoded) != self.size:
358            raise ValueError(
359                f"Encoded input size ({len(encoded)}) does not match encoder size ({self.size})"
360            )
361
362        results: dict[
363            str, list[tuple[tuple[int, int], float | None, float]] | tuple[float | None, float]
364        ] = {"frequencies": []}
365
366        for range_index, freq_range in enumerate(self._frequency_ranges):
367            start_freq, stop_freq = freq_range
368            current_res = self._resolutions_in_ranges[range_index]
369            current_sparsity = self._sparsity_in_ranges[range_index]
370
371            freq_encoder = RandomDistributedScalarEncoder(
372                RDSEParameters(
373                    size=self._size,
374                    sparsity=current_sparsity,
375                    active_bits=0,
376                    resolution=current_res,
377                )
378            )
379
380            if candidates is not None:
381                range_candidates = [
382                    candidate for candidate in candidates if start_freq <= candidate < stop_freq
383                ]
384                if not range_candidates:
385                    range_candidates = list(range(start_freq, stop_freq))
386            else:
387                range_candidates = list(range(start_freq, stop_freq))
388            decoded_value, confidence = freq_encoder.decode(encoded, range_candidates)
389            results["frequencies"].append((freq_range, decoded_value, confidence))
390
391        magnitude_candidates: list[float] = []
392        if self._time_step > 0:
393            magnitude_candidates = list(
394                np.arange(0.0, 1.0 + self._time_step, self._time_step, dtype=float)
395            )
396
397        if magnitude_candidates:
398            magnitude_encoder = RandomDistributedScalarEncoder(
399                RDSEParameters(
400                    size=self._size,
401                    sparsity=current_sparsity,
402                    active_bits=0,
403                    resolution=current_res,
404                )
405            )
406            results["magnitude"] = magnitude_encoder.decode(encoded, magnitude_candidates)
407
408        return results

Decode a combined Fourier SDR into per-range frequency estimates and magnitude.

@dataclass
class FourierEncoderParameters: 
530@dataclass
531class FourierEncoderParameters:
532    """Class to hold fourier encodder parameters
533
534    parameters:
535        samples : total number of samples or bucket size -> N
536        size : size of the encoder output SDR
537        start_time : start time of the time interval -> integral lower bound
538        stop_time : stop time of the time interval -> integral upper bound
539        interval_size : total number of buckets in the time interval -> N
540        time_step_n : the curreent value of x at time n -> n
541
542    """
543
544    # reference to Encoder
545    encoder_class = FourierEncoder
546    """Reference to the FourierEncoder class."""
547
548    size: int = 2048
549    """The size the encoder"""
550
551    total_active_bits: int = 0
552    """Total number of active bits in the encoder output SDR."""
553
554    frequency_ranges: list[tuple[int, int]] = field(default_factory=lambda: [(0, 1024)])
555    """List of frequency ranges to find."""
556
557    active_bits_in_ranges: list[int] = field(default_factory=lambda: [])
558    """The number of active bits per frequency range in the encoder output SDR."""
559
560    sparsity_in_ranges: list[float] = field(default_factory=lambda: [0.02])
561    """The sparsity per frequency range in the encoder output SDR."""
562
563    resolutions_in_ranges: list[float] = field(default_factory=lambda: [1.0])
564    """The resolution per frequency range in the encoder output SDR."""
565
566    seed: int = 32
567    """Random seed for reproducibility."""
568
569    sensitivity_threshold: float = 0.1
570    """Magnitude threshold for considering a frequency component as a peak."""
571
572    #  time domain params
573    start_time: float = 0.0
574    """Start time of the time interval."""
575
576    stop_time: float = 1.0
577    """Stop time of the time interval."""
578
579    total_samples: int = 2048
580    """Total number of samples in the period."""

Class to hold fourier encodder parameters

parameters: samples : total number of samples or bucket size -> N size : size of the encoder output SDR start_time : start time of the time interval -> integral lower bound stop_time : stop time of the time interval -> integral upper bound interval_size : total number of buckets in the time interval -> N time_step_n : the curreent value of x at time n -> n

FourierEncoderParameters( size: int = 2048, total_active_bits: int = 0, frequency_ranges: list[tuple[int, int]] = <factory>, active_bits_in_ranges: list[int] = <factory>, sparsity_in_ranges: list[float] = <factory>, resolutions_in_ranges: list[float] = <factory>, seed: int = 32, sensitivity_threshold: float = 0.1, start_time: float = 0.0, stop_time: float = 1.0, total_samples: int = 2048)
encoder_class = <class 'FourierEncoder'>

Reference to the FourierEncoder class.

size: int = 2048

The size the encoder

total_active_bits: int = 0

Total number of active bits in the encoder output SDR.

frequency_ranges: list[tuple[int, int]]

List of frequency ranges to find.

active_bits_in_ranges: list[int]

The number of active bits per frequency range in the encoder output SDR.

sparsity_in_ranges: list[float]

The sparsity per frequency range in the encoder output SDR.

resolutions_in_ranges: list[float]

The resolution per frequency range in the encoder output SDR.

seed: int = 32

Random seed for reproducibility.

sensitivity_threshold: float = 0.1

Magnitude threshold for considering a frequency component as a peak.

start_time: float = 0.0

Start time of the time interval.

stop_time: float = 1.0

Stop time of the time interval.

total_samples: int = 2048

Total number of samples in the period.

class GeospatialEncoder(psu_capstone.encoder_layer.BaseEncoder[tuple[float, float, float] | tuple[float, float, float, float]]): 
 34class GeospatialEncoder(
 35    BaseEncoder[tuple[float, float, float] | tuple[float, float, float, float]]
 36):
 37    """Encode geospatial position and speed into coordinate-based SDRs."""
 38
 39    def __init__(
 40        self,
 41        geo_params: GeospatialParameters | None = None,
 42        coord_params: CoordinateParameters | None = None,
 43        dimensions: list[int] | None = None,
 44    ):
 45
 46        self._geo_params = (
 47            copy.deepcopy(geo_params) if geo_params is not None else GeospatialParameters()
 48        )
 49        self._coord_params = (
 50            copy.deepcopy(coord_params) if coord_params is not None else CoordinateParameters()
 51        )
 52
 53        coord_params = CoordinateParameters(
 54            n=self._coord_params.n,
 55            w=self._coord_params.w,
 56            seed=self._coord_params.seed,
 57            max_radius=self._geo_params.max_radius,
 58            dims=3 if self._geo_params.use_altitude else 2,
 59        )
 60
 61        self._encoder = CoordinateEncoder(coord_params)
 62
 63        super().__init__(self._encoder.size)
 64
 65    @override
 66    def encode(
 67        self, input_value: tuple[float, float, float] | tuple[float, float, float, float]
 68    ) -> list[int]:
 69        """Encode ``(speed, lon, lat[, alt])`` into an SDR."""
 70        if len(input_value) == 3:
 71            speed, lon, lat = input_value
 72            alt = None
 73        elif len(input_value) == 4:
 74            speed, lon, lat, alt = input_value
 75        else:
 76            raise ValueError("Expected (speed, lon, lat) or (speed, lon, lat, alt).")
 77
 78        lon = float(lon)
 79        lat = float(lat)
 80        speed = float(speed)
 81        alt = None if alt is None else float(alt)
 82
 83        lon = self._wrap_lon(lon)
 84        lat = self._clamp_lat(lat)
 85
 86        coord = self.coordinate_for_position(lon, lat, alt)
 87        radius = self.radius_for_speed(speed)
 88
 89        return self._encoder.encode((coord, radius))
 90
 91    @override
 92    def decode(
 93        self,
 94        encoded: list[int],
 95        candidates: Iterable[tuple[tuple[int, ...], int]] | None = None,
 96    ) -> tuple[tuple[float, float, Optional[float]] | None, float]:
 97        """Decode an SDR into an approximate ``(lon, lat, alt)`` tuple."""
 98
 99        best_key, conf = self._encoder.decode(encoded, candidates=candidates)
100        if best_key is None:
101            return None, 0.0
102
103        grid_coord, _radius = best_key
104        pos = self.position_for_coordinate(grid_coord)
105        return pos, conf
106
107    def coordinate_for_position(
108        self, lon: float, lat: float, alt: Optional[float]
109    ) -> tuple[int, ...]:
110        """Project geographic coordinates into integer grid coordinates."""
111
112        scale = float(self._geo_params.scale)
113
114        if self._geo_params.use_altitude and alt is not None:
115            # Mercator meters and altitude to geocentric meters
116            x, y, z = T_WGS84_TO_GEO.transform(lon, lat, float(alt))
117            coord = np.array([x, y, z], dtype=float) / float(self._geo_params.scale)
118            return tuple(int(round(v)) for v in coord)
119
120        x_m, y_m = T_WGS84_TO_MERC.transform(lon, lat)
121        coord = np.array([x_m, y_m], dtype=float) / scale
122        return tuple(int(round(v)) for v in coord)
123
124    def position_for_coordinate(
125        self, coord: tuple[int, ...]
126    ) -> tuple[float, float, Optional[float]]:
127        """Map integer grid coordinates back to geographic position."""
128        scale = float(self._geo_params.scale)
129
130        if self._geo_params.use_altitude and len(coord) == 3:
131            x, y, z = (coord[0] * scale, coord[1] * scale, coord[2] * scale)
132            lon, lat, alt = T_GEO_TO_WGS84.transform(x, y, z)
133            lon = self._wrap_lon(float(lon))
134            lat = self._clamp_lat(float(lat))
135            return lon, lat, alt
136
137        x_m, y_m = (coord[0] * scale, coord[1] * scale)
138        lon, lat = T_MERC_TO_WGS84.transform(x_m, y_m)
139        lon = self._wrap_lon(float(lon))
140        lat = self._clamp_lat(float(lat))
141        return lon, lat, None
142
143    def radius_for_speed(self, speed_mps: float) -> int:
144        """Compute coordinate radius from speed and timestep settings."""
145
146        overlap = 1.5
147        coords_per_timestep = speed_mps * float(self._geo_params.timestep)
148        r = int(round((coords_per_timestep / 2.0) * overlap))
149
150        min_r = int(math.ceil((math.sqrt(self._coord_params.w) - 1.0) / 2.0))
151
152        r = max(r, min_r)
153        r = max(0, min(r, self._geo_params.max_radius))
154        return r
155
156    @staticmethod
157    def _clamp_lat(lat: float) -> float:
158        # Web Mercator is undefinded at poles so we should clamp it
159        return max(-85.05112878, min(85.05112878, lat))
160
161    @staticmethod
162    def _wrap_lon(lon: float) -> float:
163        # wrap to [-180, 180)
164        lon = (lon + 180.0) % 360.0 - 180.0
165        return lon

Encode geospatial position and speed into coordinate-based SDRs.

GeospatialEncoder( geo_params: GeospatialParameters | None = None, coord_params: CoordinateParameters | None = None, dimensions: list[int] | None = None) 
39    def __init__(
40        self,
41        geo_params: GeospatialParameters | None = None,
42        coord_params: CoordinateParameters | None = None,
43        dimensions: list[int] | None = None,
44    ):
45
46        self._geo_params = (
47            copy.deepcopy(geo_params) if geo_params is not None else GeospatialParameters()
48        )
49        self._coord_params = (
50            copy.deepcopy(coord_params) if coord_params is not None else CoordinateParameters()
51        )
52
53        coord_params = CoordinateParameters(
54            n=self._coord_params.n,
55            w=self._coord_params.w,
56            seed=self._coord_params.seed,
57            max_radius=self._geo_params.max_radius,
58            dims=3 if self._geo_params.use_altitude else 2,
59        )
60
61        self._encoder = CoordinateEncoder(coord_params)
62
63        super().__init__(self._encoder.size)
@override
def encode( self, input_value: tuple[float, float, float] | tuple[float, float, float, float]) -> list[int]: 
65    @override
66    def encode(
67        self, input_value: tuple[float, float, float] | tuple[float, float, float, float]
68    ) -> list[int]:
69        """Encode ``(speed, lon, lat[, alt])`` into an SDR."""
70        if len(input_value) == 3:
71            speed, lon, lat = input_value
72            alt = None
73        elif len(input_value) == 4:
74            speed, lon, lat, alt = input_value
75        else:
76            raise ValueError("Expected (speed, lon, lat) or (speed, lon, lat, alt).")
77
78        lon = float(lon)
79        lat = float(lat)
80        speed = float(speed)
81        alt = None if alt is None else float(alt)
82
83        lon = self._wrap_lon(lon)
84        lat = self._clamp_lat(lat)
85
86        coord = self.coordinate_for_position(lon, lat, alt)
87        radius = self.radius_for_speed(speed)
88
89        return self._encoder.encode((coord, radius))

Encode (speed, lon, lat[, alt]) into an SDR.

@override
def decode( self, encoded: list[int], candidates: Optional[Iterable[tuple[tuple[int, ...], int]]] = None) -> tuple[tuple[float, float, typing.Optional[float]] | None, float]: 
 91    @override
 92    def decode(
 93        self,
 94        encoded: list[int],
 95        candidates: Iterable[tuple[tuple[int, ...], int]] | None = None,
 96    ) -> tuple[tuple[float, float, Optional[float]] | None, float]:
 97        """Decode an SDR into an approximate ``(lon, lat, alt)`` tuple."""
 98
 99        best_key, conf = self._encoder.decode(encoded, candidates=candidates)
100        if best_key is None:
101            return None, 0.0
102
103        grid_coord, _radius = best_key
104        pos = self.position_for_coordinate(grid_coord)
105        return pos, conf

Decode an SDR into an approximate (lon, lat, alt) tuple.

def coordinate_for_position(self, lon: float, lat: float, alt: Optional[float]) -> tuple[int, ...]: 
107    def coordinate_for_position(
108        self, lon: float, lat: float, alt: Optional[float]
109    ) -> tuple[int, ...]:
110        """Project geographic coordinates into integer grid coordinates."""
111
112        scale = float(self._geo_params.scale)
113
114        if self._geo_params.use_altitude and alt is not None:
115            # Mercator meters and altitude to geocentric meters
116            x, y, z = T_WGS84_TO_GEO.transform(lon, lat, float(alt))
117            coord = np.array([x, y, z], dtype=float) / float(self._geo_params.scale)
118            return tuple(int(round(v)) for v in coord)
119
120        x_m, y_m = T_WGS84_TO_MERC.transform(lon, lat)
121        coord = np.array([x_m, y_m], dtype=float) / scale
122        return tuple(int(round(v)) for v in coord)

Project geographic coordinates into integer grid coordinates.

def position_for_coordinate( self, coord: tuple[int, ...]) -> tuple[float, float, typing.Optional[float]]: 
124    def position_for_coordinate(
125        self, coord: tuple[int, ...]
126    ) -> tuple[float, float, Optional[float]]:
127        """Map integer grid coordinates back to geographic position."""
128        scale = float(self._geo_params.scale)
129
130        if self._geo_params.use_altitude and len(coord) == 3:
131            x, y, z = (coord[0] * scale, coord[1] * scale, coord[2] * scale)
132            lon, lat, alt = T_GEO_TO_WGS84.transform(x, y, z)
133            lon = self._wrap_lon(float(lon))
134            lat = self._clamp_lat(float(lat))
135            return lon, lat, alt
136
137        x_m, y_m = (coord[0] * scale, coord[1] * scale)
138        lon, lat = T_MERC_TO_WGS84.transform(x_m, y_m)
139        lon = self._wrap_lon(float(lon))
140        lat = self._clamp_lat(float(lat))
141        return lon, lat, None

Map integer grid coordinates back to geographic position.

def radius_for_speed(self, speed_mps: float) -> int: 
143    def radius_for_speed(self, speed_mps: float) -> int:
144        """Compute coordinate radius from speed and timestep settings."""
145
146        overlap = 1.5
147        coords_per_timestep = speed_mps * float(self._geo_params.timestep)
148        r = int(round((coords_per_timestep / 2.0) * overlap))
149
150        min_r = int(math.ceil((math.sqrt(self._coord_params.w) - 1.0) / 2.0))
151
152        r = max(r, min_r)
153        r = max(0, min(r, self._geo_params.max_radius))
154        return r

Compute coordinate radius from speed and timestep settings.

@dataclass
class GeospatialParameters: 
168@dataclass
169class GeospatialParameters:
170    """Configuration parameters for :class:`GeospatialEncoder`."""
171
172    size: int = 2048
173
174    # meters per grid unit
175    scale: float = 5.0
176
177    # seconds between readings
178    timestep: float = 1.0
179
180    # clamp output radius
181    max_radius: int = 2
182
183    use_altitude: bool = True
184
185    encoder_class = GeospatialEncoder

Configuration parameters for GeospatialEncoder.

GeospatialParameters( size: int = 2048, scale: float = 5.0, timestep: float = 1.0, max_radius: int = 2, use_altitude: bool = True)
size: int = 2048
scale: float = 5.0
timestep: float = 1.0
max_radius: int = 2
use_altitude: bool = True
encoder_class = <class 'GeospatialEncoder'>
@dataclass
class CoordinateParameters: 
181@dataclass
182class CoordinateParameters:
183    """Configuration parameters for :class:`CoordinateEncoder`."""
184
185    size: int = 2048
186    n: int = 2048
187    w: int = 25
188    seed: int = 42
189    max_radius: int = 2
190    dims: int = 2
191    use_all_neighbors: bool = False
192    encoder_class = CoordinateEncoder

Configuration parameters for CoordinateEncoder.

CoordinateParameters( size: int = 2048, n: int = 2048, w: int = 25, seed: int = 42, max_radius: int = 2, dims: int = 2, use_all_neighbors: bool = False)
size: int = 2048
n: int = 2048
w: int = 25
seed: int = 42
max_radius: int = 2
dims: int = 2
use_all_neighbors: bool = False
encoder_class = <class 'CoordinateEncoder'>
class CoordinateEncoder(psu_capstone.encoder_layer.BaseEncoder[tuple[tuple[int, ...] | list[int], int]]): 
 16class CoordinateEncoder(BaseEncoder[tuple[tuple[int, ...] | list[int], int]]):
 17    """Encode integer grid coordinates and radius into an SDR."""
 18
 19    def __init__(
 20        self, parameters: CoordinateParameters | None = None, dimensions: list[int] | None = None
 21    ):
 22
 23        self._parameters = (
 24            copy.deepcopy(parameters) if parameters is not None else CoordinateParameters()
 25        )
 26
 27        self._n = self._parameters.n
 28        self._w = self._parameters.w
 29        self._seed = self._parameters.seed
 30        self._dims = self._parameters.dims
 31        self._max_radius = self._parameters.max_radius
 32        self._use_all_neighbors = self._parameters.use_all_neighbors
 33
 34        enc_params = RDSEParameters(
 35            size=self._n,
 36            active_bits=self._w,
 37            sparsity=0.0,
 38            radius=0.0,
 39            resolution=1.0 / self._n,
 40            category=False,
 41            seed=self._seed,
 42        )
 43
 44        self._encoder = RandomDistributedScalarEncoder(enc_params)
 45        self._overlap = self._encoder._overlap
 46        self._encoding_cache: dict[tuple[tuple[int, ...], int], list[int]] = {}
 47        self._max_neighbors = (2 * self._parameters.max_radius + 1) ** self._dims
 48
 49        if self._parameters.use_all_neighbors:
 50            # all neighbors
 51            self._size = self._max_neighbors * self._n
 52        else:
 53            # all winners
 54            self._size = self._w * self._n
 55
 56        super().__init__(self._size)
 57
 58    @override
 59    def encode(self, input_value: tuple[tuple[int, ...] | list[int], int]) -> list[int]:
 60        """Encode ``(coordinate, radius)`` into a fixed-size binary SDR."""
 61        return self.register_encoding(input_value)
 62
 63    def _compute_encoding(self, key: tuple[tuple[int, ...], int]) -> list[int]:
 64        coordinate, radius = key
 65
 66        if len(coordinate) != self._dims:
 67            raise ValueError(f"Expected coordinate with dims={self._dims}, got {len(coordinate)}")
 68
 69        r = int(radius)
 70        if r < 0 or r > self._max_radius:
 71            raise ValueError(f"Radius must be in [0, {self._max_radius}], got {r}")
 72
 73        neighbors = self._neighbors(coordinate, r)
 74
 75        out: list[int] = []
 76        if self._use_all_neighbors:
 77            for c in neighbors:
 78                v = self._coord_to_unit_float(c)
 79                out.extend(int(b) for b in self._encoder.encode(v))
 80
 81            expected = self._max_neighbors * self._n
 82        else:
 83            winners = self._topwcoordinates(neighbors, self._w)
 84
 85            for c in winners:
 86                v = self._coord_to_unit_float(c)
 87                out.extend(int(b) for b in self._encoder.encode(v))
 88
 89            expected = self._w * self._n
 90
 91        if len(out) < expected:
 92            out.extend([0] * (expected - len(out)))
 93        else:
 94            out = out[:expected]
 95
 96        return out
 97
 98    def register_encoding(
 99        self, input_value: tuple[tuple[int, ...] | list[int], int], encoded: list[int] | None = None
100    ) -> list[int]:
101        """Cache and return the encoding for a coordinate/radius key.
102
103        ? why do we need to this to be tuple of tuple, why not just tuple[list[int], int] for coordinate?
104        I think it maybe less confusing to simply use the tuple[list[int], int] as the key, since the coordinate is already a tuple of ints, and the radius is an int, so we can just use that directly as the key without needing to wrap it in another tuple. This would also make the code simpler and easier to read, since we wouldn't need to unpack the coordinate from the outer tuple every time we want to access it.
105
106        """
107        coordinate, radius = input_value
108
109        key = (tuple(int(v) for v in coordinate), int(radius))
110        vector = encoded if encoded is not None else self._compute_encoding(key)
111
112        if len(vector) != self._size:
113            raise ValueError("Stored encoding must match encoder size")
114
115        self._encoding_cache[key] = vector
116        return vector
117
118    @staticmethod
119    def _neighbors(coordinate, radius):
120        ranges = (range(int(n) - radius, int(n) + radius + 1) for n in coordinate)
121        return itertools.product(*ranges)
122
123    @classmethod
124    def _topwcoordinates(cls, coordinates, w):
125        scored = [(cls._order_for_coordinate(c), c) for c in coordinates]
126
127        scored.sort(key=lambda x: x[0])
128        return [c for _, c in scored[-w:]]
129
130    @staticmethod
131    def _order_for_coordinate(coordinate) -> float:
132        s = ",".join(str(int(v)) for v in coordinate)
133        h = mmh3.hash(s, signed=False)
134        return h / 2**32
135
136    def _coord_to_unit_float(self, coordinate) -> float:
137        s = ",".join(str(int(v)) for v in coordinate)
138        h = mmh3.hash(s, seed=self._seed, signed=False)
139        return h / 2**32
140
141    @override
142    def decode(
143        self,
144        encoded: list[int],
145        candidates: Iterable[tuple[tuple[int, ...], int]] | None = None,
146    ) -> tuple[tuple[tuple[int, ...], int] | None, float]:
147        """Decode an SDR to the nearest cached coordinate/radius candidate."""
148        if len(encoded) != self.size:
149            raise ValueError(
150                f"Encoded input size ({len(encoded)}) does not match encoder size ({self.size})"
151            )
152
153        search_keys = (
154            list(candidates) if candidates is not None else list(self._encoding_cache.keys())
155        )
156        if not search_keys:
157            raise ValueError("No candidate encodings are available for decoding")
158
159        best_key = None
160        best_overlap = -1
161
162        for key in search_keys:
163            candidate_encoding = self._encoding_cache.get(key)
164            if candidate_encoding is None:
165                candidate_encoding = self.register_encoding(key)
166
167            overlap = self._overlap(encoded, candidate_encoding)
168            if overlap > best_overlap:
169                best_overlap = overlap
170                best_key = key
171
172        if self._use_all_neighbors:
173            blocks = (2 * self._max_radius + 1) ** self._dims
174        else:
175            blocks = self._w
176        expected_ones = blocks * self._w
177        confidence = best_overlap / expected_ones if expected_ones > 0 else 0.0
178        return best_key, confidence

Encode integer grid coordinates and radius into an SDR.

CoordinateEncoder( parameters: CoordinateParameters | None = None, dimensions: list[int] | None = None) 
19    def __init__(
20        self, parameters: CoordinateParameters | None = None, dimensions: list[int] | None = None
21    ):
22
23        self._parameters = (
24            copy.deepcopy(parameters) if parameters is not None else CoordinateParameters()
25        )
26
27        self._n = self._parameters.n
28        self._w = self._parameters.w
29        self._seed = self._parameters.seed
30        self._dims = self._parameters.dims
31        self._max_radius = self._parameters.max_radius
32        self._use_all_neighbors = self._parameters.use_all_neighbors
33
34        enc_params = RDSEParameters(
35            size=self._n,
36            active_bits=self._w,
37            sparsity=0.0,
38            radius=0.0,
39            resolution=1.0 / self._n,
40            category=False,
41            seed=self._seed,
42        )
43
44        self._encoder = RandomDistributedScalarEncoder(enc_params)
45        self._overlap = self._encoder._overlap
46        self._encoding_cache: dict[tuple[tuple[int, ...], int], list[int]] = {}
47        self._max_neighbors = (2 * self._parameters.max_radius + 1) ** self._dims
48
49        if self._parameters.use_all_neighbors:
50            # all neighbors
51            self._size = self._max_neighbors * self._n
52        else:
53            # all winners
54            self._size = self._w * self._n
55
56        super().__init__(self._size)
@override
def encode(self, input_value: tuple[tuple[int, ...] | list[int], int]) -> list[int]: 
58    @override
59    def encode(self, input_value: tuple[tuple[int, ...] | list[int], int]) -> list[int]:
60        """Encode ``(coordinate, radius)`` into a fixed-size binary SDR."""
61        return self.register_encoding(input_value)

Encode (coordinate, radius) into a fixed-size binary SDR.

def register_encoding( self, input_value: tuple[tuple[int, ...] | list[int], int], encoded: list[int] | None = None) -> list[int]: 
 98    def register_encoding(
 99        self, input_value: tuple[tuple[int, ...] | list[int], int], encoded: list[int] | None = None
100    ) -> list[int]:
101        """Cache and return the encoding for a coordinate/radius key.
102
103        ? why do we need to this to be tuple of tuple, why not just tuple[list[int], int] for coordinate?
104        I think it maybe less confusing to simply use the tuple[list[int], int] as the key, since the coordinate is already a tuple of ints, and the radius is an int, so we can just use that directly as the key without needing to wrap it in another tuple. This would also make the code simpler and easier to read, since we wouldn't need to unpack the coordinate from the outer tuple every time we want to access it.
105
106        """
107        coordinate, radius = input_value
108
109        key = (tuple(int(v) for v in coordinate), int(radius))
110        vector = encoded if encoded is not None else self._compute_encoding(key)
111
112        if len(vector) != self._size:
113            raise ValueError("Stored encoding must match encoder size")
114
115        self._encoding_cache[key] = vector
116        return vector

Cache and return the encoding for a coordinate/radius key.

? why do we need to this to be tuple of tuple, why not just tuple[list[int], int] for coordinate? I think it maybe less confusing to simply use the tuple[list[int], int] as the key, since the coordinate is already a tuple of ints, and the radius is an int, so we can just use that directly as the key without needing to wrap it in another tuple. This would also make the code simpler and easier to read, since we wouldn't need to unpack the coordinate from the outer tuple every time we want to access it.

@override
def decode( self, encoded: list[int], candidates: Optional[Iterable[tuple[tuple[int, ...], int]]] = None) -> tuple[tuple[tuple[int, ...], int] | None, float]: 
141    @override
142    def decode(
143        self,
144        encoded: list[int],
145        candidates: Iterable[tuple[tuple[int, ...], int]] | None = None,
146    ) -> tuple[tuple[tuple[int, ...], int] | None, float]:
147        """Decode an SDR to the nearest cached coordinate/radius candidate."""
148        if len(encoded) != self.size:
149            raise ValueError(
150                f"Encoded input size ({len(encoded)}) does not match encoder size ({self.size})"
151            )
152
153        search_keys = (
154            list(candidates) if candidates is not None else list(self._encoding_cache.keys())
155        )
156        if not search_keys:
157            raise ValueError("No candidate encodings are available for decoding")
158
159        best_key = None
160        best_overlap = -1
161
162        for key in search_keys:
163            candidate_encoding = self._encoding_cache.get(key)
164            if candidate_encoding is None:
165                candidate_encoding = self.register_encoding(key)
166
167            overlap = self._overlap(encoded, candidate_encoding)
168            if overlap > best_overlap:
169                best_overlap = overlap
170                best_key = key
171
172        if self._use_all_neighbors:
173            blocks = (2 * self._max_radius + 1) ** self._dims
174        else:
175            blocks = self._w
176        expected_ones = blocks * self._w
177        confidence = best_overlap / expected_ones if expected_ones > 0 else 0.0
178        return best_key, confidence

Decode an SDR to the nearest cached coordinate/radius candidate.
